The bottleneck already moved. A GPU that computes in nanoseconds and waits microseconds for data is a stranded asset. At 1.6T speeds, copper runs out of physics. The constraint on AI is no longer how fast you can think. It's how fast you can move what you thought. Jensen has now said it twice in three months. At GTC in March: "Is copper going to still be important? The answer is yes... Are you going to scale up optical? Yes. Are you going to scale out optical? Yes... We need a lot more capacity for copper. We need a lot more capacity for optics. We need a lot more capacity for CPO." Last week at Computex, on Marvell's stage: "Optics where you must, copper where you can." Then he called Marvell the next trillion-dollar company and the optical complex repriced within days. The same keynote put a date on the handoff: 200G per lane is the last generation where copper is sufficient. After that, optics takes the rack. Translation: not copper OR light. Copper now, light next, unprecedented amounts of both. 🔥 The chain is unavoidable: AI tokens are profitable → more GPUs → more bandwidth → copper hits its wall → photonics becomes the chokepoint. And the smart money stopped debating. Follow the closed deals: → $NVDA has committed at least $6.5B to photonics in three months: $2B into Lumentum, $2B into Coherent, a $500M stake in Corning, and a piece of Ayar Labs' $500M round. Direct investments to secure its own light supply. → $MRVL paid $3.25B for Celestial AI, up to $5.5B with milestones, to build what its CEO calls a silicon photonics powerhouse. → $CRDO closed DustPhotonics two weeks ago. Ciena bought CPO startup Nubis for $270M. North of $10B of strategic capital locked up one supply chain in under a year. Capital like that doesn't chase a theme. It secures a bottleneck. LAYER 1: WAFER. Every laser starts as a crystal. 🟠 $AXTI: the InP substrate leader. The first chokepoint in the stack. 🟡 $IQE: compound-semi epiwafers feeding the laser makers. Speculative, but structurally upstream. LAYER 2: LIGHT. Photons don't make themselves. 🟠 $LITE: revenue +90% YoY last quarter to $808M. EML shipments doubled and management says demand still exceeds supply across EMLs, pump lasers, and transceivers. NVIDIA just wired them $2B. OCS backlog past $400M plus a multi-hundred-million CPO order for 2027. 🟢 $SIVEF (Stockholm: SIVE): the external light source. CPO does not emit its own light. Every optical engine needs a continuous-wave InP laser feeding it, and that is the layer you cannot engineer around. ELS modules with POET hit production readiness end of this year. Disclosure: long. 🟣 $POET: the optical engine wildcard. Its Optical Interposer pairs with Sivers' lasers on external light sources for CPO, with a LITEON module deal stacked on top. Binary commercialization, real architecture. LAYER 3: OPTICS AND MODULES. Where light meets the rack. 🟠 $COHR: the volume anchor in transceivers, holding NVIDIA's other $2B check. 🔵 $AAOI: Q1 revenue +51% to a record $151M, datacenter revenue more than doubled, $124M of 800G orders plus a $200M+ 1.6T order in hand. Scaling Texas capacity toward 500K+ units a month by year-end, targeting $1B+ revenue this year. Domestic supply while everyone fights over offshore. Disclosure: long. 🟠 $FN: the foundry of optics. When Fabrinet is building, the orders already exist. THE INTERCONNECT: the layer the rack cannot route around. 🔵 $CRDO: just closed DustPhotonics. SerDes → DSP → silicon photonics → system integration, one company, 800G through 3.2T. Electrical AND optical, end to end. FY26 revenue tripled to $1.34B at 68% gross margin. The toll booth on both roads. Disclosure: long. 🟠 $MRVL: $3.25B for Celestial AI, and Jensen's trillion-dollar nod on the Computex stage. 🟠 $AVGO: switch silicon, optical DSPs, CPO engines. They define the socket. 🟠 $ANET: the AI spine. 100K-GPU clusters get stitched together in light. LAYER 4: PACKAGING, FIBER, FOUNDRY. Where photons get industrialized. 🔵 $TSEM: the neutral silicon photonics foundry. Prints wafers for whoever wins. 🟣 $LPKF: glass-substrate packaging for glass-based CPO. Real technology, binary commercialization. 🟠 $GLW: AI racks demand several times the fiber density of legacy cloud, and NVIDIA just took a $500M stake. Corning sells density. LAYER 5: TEST AND THE ANALOG UNDERLAYER. Complexity is a tax paid in validation. 🔵 $AEHR: silicon photonics test, ramping with the cycle. ' 🔵 $VIAV: every 800G and 1.6T transceiver gets validated before it ships. The gate the market prices like an accessory. 🔵 $SMTC: the drivers and TIAs that fire the lasers. Sits directly under the LPO trade. 🔵 $MTSI: the high-speed analog behind 1.6T engines. 🟠 $CIEN: transport. Even long-haul is buying light. 💡The counter-thesis, because every map needs one. The honest debate on this stack is whether these are genuine bottleneck assets or cyclical optics suppliers enjoying peak demand at peak multiples. Lumentum's May print showed +90% growth with the stock up roughly 1,400% over the prior year at a triple-digit trailing multiple. That is a price for perfection. Most of these names live or die on a handful of hyperscaler capex lines, and one digestion quarter hits the whole stack at once. CPO timing has already slipped once. Architecture risk is real: LPO, CPO, and stretched copper are still fighting for the same sockets. The cycle is real. So is the gravity. 🔥 But the bears have to explain one thing: $NVDA, $MRVL, $CRDO, and $CIEN just spent over $10B securing this supply chain with their own balance sheets. The people with the best information are paying up for the layers. The market owns the top of this stack. The asymmetry is at the edges: wafer, light, packaging, test. Own the layers, not the logo. Bookmark this for the weekend. Then tag the one investor you know who's still all compute and no interconnect. 👀"}}}, "rest_id": "2065518398872961392", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "96634", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2066309157569851897", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "98", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text: I'm old enough to remember when buying $RIVN was going to be the next big thing.  Which of the photonics stocks is the next Rivian or are they all winners? https://t.co/P02o4huns4

FÁBRICAS EN EL ESPACIO

$SPCX 🚀

En un futuro, con costos de transporte espacial bajos, la fabricación y servicios en órbita se vuelven viables y rentables, especialmente para productos de alto valor donde la ausencia de gravedad mejora significativamente la calidad, reduce defectos por convección/sedimentación o permite estructuras imposibles en Tierra.

Fabricación de Materiales Avanzados

🔸Fibras ópticas ZBLAN (o similares): En microgravedad se producen fibras con mucho menos defectos cristalinos y pérdidas de señal (hasta 10-100 veces mejores que en Tierra). Ideales para telecomunicaciones de alta velocidad, láseres médicos y sensores. Empresas como Flawless Photonics, FOMS y Made In Space ya han demostrado producción en la ISS (kilómetros de fibra).

🔸Cristales de proteínas y farmacéuticos: Crecimiento de cristales más grandes, uniformes y puros para drogas (ej. ritonavir, anticuerpos monoclonales). Mejora la formulación de medicamentos, permite nuevas terapias y acelera el descubrimiento. Varda Space ya retornó productos fabricados en órbita.

🔸Semiconductores y chips: Fabricación con menor contaminación por gravedad, mejor uniformidad en dopaje y cristales. Potencial para superar limitaciones terrestres en rendimiento y escalabilidad (apoyado por CHIPS Act).

🔸Aleaciones y materiales compuestos metálicos: Mezclas más homogéneas sin sedimentación (ej. espumas metálicas, superaleaciones). Mejores propiedades estructurales para aeronáutica, energía y herramientas.

🔸Nanomateriales y vidrios especializados: Producción de nanomateriales, vidrios exóticos o películas delgadas con propiedades únicas gracias al vacío y ausencia de convección.

🔸Impresión 3D y fabricación aditiva en órbita: Partes complejas sin soportes, estructuras grandes (vigas, paneles) optimizadas para espacio. Reduce masa lanzada y permite reparaciones in-situ o construcción de hábitats/telescopios.

🔸Bioprinting y tejidos/regenerativa: Impresión de órganos, retinas artificiales o tejidos en 3D sin colapso por gravedad. LambdaVision y otros trabajan en membranas para restaurar visión.

Servicios y Otras Aplicaciones

🔹Ensamblaje y mantenimiento de grandes estructuras: Construcción de estaciones espaciales, paneles solares orbitales, telescopios gigantes o estaciones de energía solar (Space-Based Solar Power). Más fácil manejar componentes masivos sin peso.

🔹Depósitos de combustible y logística orbital: Fabricación/refinamiento de propelentes o estaciones de servicio para naves (refueling). Clave para misiones profundas.

🔹Investigación y testing de materiales: Laboratorios permanentes para probar comportamientos en microgravedad (fluidos, combustión, biología) que aceleran innovación terrestre.

🔹Producción de productos biológicos y dispositivos médicos: Cultivo de células, modelado de enfermedades o dispositivos implantables con mejor control de fluidos.

🔹Turismo y experiencias en microgravedad: fábricas orbitales podrían ofrecer tours o entornos controlados para entrenamiento/research.

🔹Reciclaje y economía circular orbital: Reprocesar desechos espaciales o satélites en nuevos materiales, reduciendo basura orbital y costos.

Consideraciones Generales

☑️Ventajas clave: Ausencia de convección/burbujas/sedimentación, vacío natural, energía solar abundante y disipación de calor fácil. Ideal para productos de alto valor por kg (fibras ópticas o fármacos justifican fácilmente el transporte).

☑️Desafíos actuales: Retorno seguro a Tierra, automatización/robótica (para reducir tripulación), regulaciones (FDA para fármacos) y escalabilidad. Con costos bajos, se abre un mercado comercial masivo.

☑️Empresas pioneras: Axiom Space, Redwire/Made In Space, Varda, Flawless Photonics, Sierra Space, etc.

Esta lista se basa en avances reales y proyecciones hacia 2030+. Con costos por kg cayendo drásticamente, pasará de nicho a industria transformadora, similar a cómo la reutilización revolucionó los lanzamientos.

text:
$XFAB - The next $TSEM for Europe?

Largest pure-play SiC foundry globally being the only high-volume manufacturing capacity in the US.
 
6 fabs worldwide, and over 400 customers. (#1 foundry supplier for 12 of the top 20)

Sounds incredibly strong at first. But don't be fooled. We are going deeper.

Today, it looks clear that two cycles could be the major upside for this company: power semiconductors and silicon photonics.

1. Power Semis shows direct exposure to $NVDA 800 VDC: $NVTS (heavily relies on them for SiC), and also partners with $POWI and Lite-On.

2. Photonics exposure is through photonixFAB consortium, and three ongoing major projects in development (high-volume production in 2028). $NOK and $NVDA are active evaluators (not confirmed customers yet).

There is also explicit backing through the US and European CHIPS Acts which validates their strategic importance for the West. 

Their tech covers a wide spectrum of CMOS, SOI, SiC, GaN, MEMs, and MTP. They operate on 200 mm wafers and node ranging from 110nm-350nm. They don't compete with customers on IC design, assembly, test or IC sales - they are a pure-play foundry. Partnerships include $IQE, $SOI, Cadence. 

Financially, it has delivered 17% CAGR over the last 5 years. Recent Q1 numbers show revenue growth was down 2%, and that the business is split into automotive (down), industrial (up 32% YoY) and medical (up). Balance sheet is quite stable with $145M in cash and $291M in net debt. 

Melexis (automotive exposure customer) accounts for ~40% of their revenue. Strong baseline but also a concentration factor.

Now here goes comes MY take and view. 

> Technology gap compared to $TSEM: For power semis, their 200mm wafer platform is excellent for high-volume manufacturing. However, for the advanced silicon photonics cycle, tier-1 players prefer migrating toward 300mm wafers to lower optical losses and shrink component footprints. This shows X-FAB is still not the absolute best or preferred platform for the premium photonics cycle yet.

> Growth upside looks heavily weighted towards photonics. Power semis will add solid revenue, but it won't be as explosive as photonics. One reason is because other IDMs like $IFX, $STM, $ON, $WOLF own their own internal fabs and don't rely on X-FAB. Shows they are not the only one in the game or as significant in town for the power semi cycle. We also don't have a lot more details on their three main ongoing photonic projects. So it is hard to be extremely bullish upfront.

> 2026 is going to be a year of steady recovery, and until mid-2027 it doesn't look like the company will show significant revenue growth. Recent Q1 call also looked more bearish than bullish, and it confirmed these timelines. Plenty of time to accumulate shares for those who wish to. Would I suggest that though? Keep reading.

> The downside risk is still really low because Melexis (major revenue stream) is guiding sales growth for H2'26, and with $XFAB at ~1x sales and $1B market cap, the upside is way more here. Seriously way more.

Ultimately, this stock has immense potential. Would definitely be my asymmetrical pick over others that have already run so much like $TSEM, $GFS, $UMC, etc. 

It looks like a 1 to 2 year long-term play, and not a quick few-months gain stock. If timelines compress, execution shows better than expected and they continue to improve on their technology sooner, we could easily see the narrative shift rapidly. This looks like something which I am optimistic on happening because that's how quick the AI infrastructure market is going forward overall. Every few months, it just surprises us.

I feel $XFAB can really go 10x or even more in the next few years, but this company needs to execute really well. It still hasn't earned the "next $TSEM" tag, as they aren't worthy of it today.

How would I play this?

- Very worth buying a decent amount of shares here, and holding for the next 2 years. Mainly because upside is 10x+ if things fall in place.

- But I wouldn't go all in at once. Rather build my position slowly and continue to accumulate over time as the story plays out.

NFA. DYOR.

Largest pure-play SiC foundry globally being the only high-volume manufacturing capacity in the US. 6 fabs worldwide, and over 400 customers. (#1 foundry supplier for 12 of the top 20) Sounds incredibly strong at first. But don't be fooled. We are going deeper. Today, it looks clear that two cycles could be the major upside for this company: power semiconductors and silicon photonics. 1. Power Semis shows direct exposure to $NVDA 800 VDC: $NVTS (heavily relies on them for SiC), and also partners with $POWI and Lite-On. 2. Photonics exposure is through photonixFAB consortium, and three ongoing major projects in development (high-volume production in 2028). $NOK and $NVDA are active evaluators (not confirmed customers yet). There is also explicit backing through the US and European CHIPS Acts which validates their strategic importance for the West. Their tech covers a wide spectrum of CMOS, SOI, SiC, GaN, MEMs, and MTP. They operate on 200 mm wafers and node ranging from 110nm-350nm. They don't compete with customers on IC design, assembly, test or IC sales - they are a pure-play foundry. Partnerships include $IQE, $SOI, Cadence. Financially, it has delivered 17% CAGR over the last 5 years. Recent Q1 numbers show revenue growth was down 2%, and that the business is split into automotive (down), industrial (up 32% YoY) and medical (up). Balance sheet is quite stable with $145M in cash and $291M in net debt. Melexis (automotive exposure customer) accounts for ~40% of their revenue. Strong baseline but also a concentration factor. Now here goes comes MY take and view. > Technology gap compared to $TSEM: For power semis, their 200mm wafer platform is excellent for high-volume manufacturing. However, for the advanced silicon photonics cycle, tier-1 players prefer migrating toward 300mm wafers to lower optical losses and shrink component footprints. This shows X-FAB is still not the absolute best or preferred platform for the premium photonics cycle yet. > Growth upside looks heavily weighted towards photonics. Power semis will add solid revenue, but it won't be as explosive as photonics. One reason is because other IDMs like $IFX, $STM, $ON, $WOLF own their own internal fabs and don't rely on X-FAB. Shows they are not the only one in the game or as significant in town for the power semi cycle. We also don't have a lot more details on their three main ongoing photonic projects. So it is hard to be extremely bullish upfront. > 2026 is going to be a year of steady recovery, and until mid-2027 it doesn't look like the company will show significant revenue growth. Recent Q1 call also looked more bearish than bullish, and it confirmed these timelines. Plenty of time to accumulate shares for those who wish to. Would I suggest that though? Keep reading. > The downside risk is still really low because Melexis (major revenue stream) is guiding sales growth for H2'26, and with $XFAB at ~1x sales and $1B market cap, the upside is way more here. Seriously way more. Ultimately, this stock has immense potential. Would definitely be my asymmetrical pick over others that have already run so much like $TSEM, $GFS, $UMC, etc. It looks like a 1 to 2 year long-term play, and not a quick few-months gain stock. If timelines compress, execution shows better than expected and they continue to improve on their technology sooner, we could easily see the narrative shift rapidly. This looks like something which I am optimistic on happening because that's how quick the AI infrastructure market is going forward overall. Every few months, it just surprises us. I feel $XFAB can really go 10x or even more in the next few years, but this company needs to execute really well. It still hasn't earned the "next $TSEM" tag, as they aren't worthy of it today. How would I play this? - Very worth buying a decent amount of shares here, and holding for the next 2 years. Mainly because upside is 10x+ if things fall in place. - But I wouldn't go all in at once. Rather build my position slowly and continue to accumulate over time as the story plays out. NFA. DYOR."}}}, "rest_id": "2066281077564383511", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "30777", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

$AAOI seeing heavy upside call flow

Largest order was the 177.5C block (~$611K), with additional buying in 200C/210C/280C/300C strikes

One put sweep at 150P showed up, but overall this is a clear bullish continuation bet

Recent catalyst mix includes AI/photonics momentum, analyst upgrades, and earnings-driven attention

text:
My core holdings right now:

AI infra / neocloud — $NBIS, IREN, NVDA
Fintech / software — HOOD, NOW, CRWD, RDDT
Memory — MU, DRAM
AI power / grid — VST, CEG, VRT, FLNC
Photonics / optical — MRVL, LITE, GLW, COHR
Metals hedge — GLD, SLV calls

Changes since last portfolio post:
Added GLD/SLV call basket.
Added/pressed some NVDA, NBIS, META, NOW, AMZN/VRT call exposure.
Trimmed IREN.
SG is out.
SOXL/SOXS are not core — just tactical trading junk, not a thesis.

Most of the real research here is still around AI infra bottlenecks: neocloud power, memory, photonics, and grid capacity.

But the actual book is broader than this account makes it look: software/fintech and a metals hedge matter too.

The honest state: winners are working, but options/cash leverage is high enough that I'm not trying to broad-add into green tape. New money has to earn it with proof or a pullback.

We'll see which ones earn their way in

text:
“Technical Analysis is Trader’s Astrology” 

Serenity’s Sharp Critique on What Really Moves Markets "White-Haired Stock God" Serenity has sparked debate with a bold take: Technical Analysis (TA) functions more like astrology for traders than a reliable predictive tool. She argues it’s primarily confirmation bias combined with crowd psychology, useful for gauging sentiment and timing entries but not the core driver of prices. Her Key Points: Massive stock surges are rarely caused by chart patterns (golden crosses, dragon candles, etc.).
They are driven by fundamentals and forward expectations. $SIVE +1900%: Market repricing future revenue from $JBL and $GFS catalysts.
$AXTI +8000%: Industry logic including indium phosphide substrates, photonics demand, export controls, and ASP dynamics.

Serenity’s Advice:
TA can help identify psychological entry points, but sustainable price trends are determined by: Industry theme linkages
Earnings expectation changes
Macro environment
Financial report quality
Circulating share structure (float)

Long-term stock potential should be judged by fundamentals and capital structure, not “chart faith. ”Why This Resonates:
In a market full of noise — especially post major IPOs like SpaceX — focusing on real catalysts (revenue growth, competitive advantages, macro tailwinds) provides a clearer edge than pattern chasing.

Investment Takeaway:
This is a timely reminder for traders and investors alike. While charts can be entertaining and occasionally helpful for short-term psychology, the biggest winners come from understanding the underlying business story and capital flows. Bottom Line: Serenity urges a return to basics. In the long run, fundamentals and capital structure trump technical patterns. A healthy dose of skepticism toward “chart magic” can protect capital and improve decision-making. Strong words from a respected voice in the community. 

Do you agree that TA is mostly astrology, or does it still play a key role in your strategy? Drop your thoughts below!

text:
Cheaper tokens ≠ less compute.  
They trigger more compute demand.  

This is Jevons Paradox in AI: cheaper intelligence causes total consumption to explode as new use cases, agentic workflows, and broader adoption become viable.  

Model giants like OpenAI face real price-war pressure. But the picks-and-shovels layer (power, optics, platforms) wins on volume. $IREN , $AAOI , $PLTR and many more are set up beautifully.  

Core insight from recent discussions (Nebius/20VC with Roman Chernin, BG2 Pod, Jordi Visser): every time cost-per-token or cost-per-unit intelligence drops, demand rises, not because people use less, but because they use far more. Previously uneconomic tasks become viable, complex multi-step agentic workflows scale, and new markets open.  

Nebius saw some of its best sales weeks right after major cheap open-source model drops. The market isn’t near its ceiling.  

Jordi Visser laid it out clearly: price compression and commoditization at the model layer (OpenAI/Anthropic facing drastic cuts, 80% of workloads shifting to far cheaper models) versus real inflation and bottlenecks at the physical layer (power, materials, networking, optics). Demand for intelligence is near-infinite. Cheaper per-token pricing unlocks the floodgates on total tokens consumed.  

Who benefits? Model-layer players can get squeezed on margins even as headline revenue grows. The infrastructure and enablement layer feasts on the volume explosion:  

• $IREN — Sustainable power, land, and AI/HPC cloud pivot. Power is the ultimate bottleneck.
• $AAOI — Optical transceivers and photonics for AI data centers. Hyperscalers are ordering 800G+ modules in volume as faster, power-efficient connectivity becomes non-negotiable.  
• $PLTR — Enterprise AI platforms (AIP/Gotham). As agentic workflows and production deployments explode, companies need real software to deploy, govern, and scale AI not just raw GPUs.  

Many more names across power, cooling, networking, and specialized infra will ride the same wave.  

@BG2Pod highlighted the bullish math: monetization per gigawatt is rising, not falling. Demand outstrips supply. Inference revenue is already running well over $200B annualized and climbing fast. Agents will multiply token consumption further. Cheaper tokens plus efficiency gains expand what’s possible within power envelopes, driving more overall buildout.  

This is why the “AI mid-cycle slowdown” narrative is incomplete. It’s healthy digestion and rotation inside a secular bull market. Capital is rotating toward the real beneficiaries of the physical buildout and the software layer that turns raw compute into usable intelligence at scale.  

The compute market remains early. We’re still in the first innings of widespread agentic adoption and enterprise transformation.  

Cheaper intelligence democratizes access → more users and businesses adopt → agents multiply usage per task → total token/compute demand detonates. Model giants feel price pressure. Infra and platform winners feel the volume tailwind.

They trigger more compute demand. This is Jevons Paradox in AI: cheaper intelligence causes total consumption to explode as new use cases, agentic workflows, and broader adoption become viable. Model giants like OpenAI face real price-war pressure. But the picks-and-shovels layer (power, optics, platforms) wins on volume. $IREN , $AAOI , $PLTR and many more are set up beautifully. Core insight from recent discussions (Nebius/20VC with Roman Chernin, BG2 Pod, Jordi Visser): every time cost-per-token or cost-per-unit intelligence drops, demand rises, not because people use less, but because they use far more. Previously uneconomic tasks become viable, complex multi-step agentic workflows scale, and new markets open. Nebius saw some of its best sales weeks right after major cheap open-source model drops. The market isn’t near its ceiling. Jordi Visser laid it out clearly: price compression and commoditization at the model layer (OpenAI/Anthropic facing drastic cuts, 80% of workloads shifting to far cheaper models) versus real inflation and bottlenecks at the physical layer (power, materials, networking, optics). Demand for intelligence is near-infinite. Cheaper per-token pricing unlocks the floodgates on total tokens consumed. Who benefits? Model-layer players can get squeezed on margins even as headline revenue grows. The infrastructure and enablement layer feasts on the volume explosion: • $IREN — Sustainable power, land, and AI/HPC cloud pivot. Power is the ultimate bottleneck. • $AAOI — Optical transceivers and photonics for AI data centers. Hyperscalers are ordering 800G+ modules in volume as faster, power-efficient connectivity becomes non-negotiable. • $PLTR — Enterprise AI platforms (AIP/Gotham). As agentic workflows and production deployments explode, companies need real software to deploy, govern, and scale AI not just raw GPUs. Many more names across power, cooling, networking, and specialized infra will ride the same wave. @BG2Pod highlighted the bullish math: monetization per gigawatt is rising, not falling. Demand outstrips supply. Inference revenue is already running well over $200B annualized and climbing fast. Agents will multiply token consumption further. Cheaper tokens plus efficiency gains expand what’s possible within power envelopes, driving more overall buildout. This is why the “AI mid-cycle slowdown” narrative is incomplete. It’s healthy digestion and rotation inside a secular bull market. Capital is rotating toward the real beneficiaries of the physical buildout and the software layer that turns raw compute into usable intelligence at scale. The compute market remains early. We’re still in the first innings of widespread agentic adoption and enterprise transformation. Cheaper intelligence democratizes access → more users and businesses adopt → agents multiply usage per task → total token/compute demand detonates. Model giants feel price pressure. Infra and platform winners feel the volume tailwind."}}}, "rest_id": "2066252039068262859", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "335", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

の構図として読む方が本質的です。
現在の光技術競争は、
NTT → 通信網全体を光化する IOWN/APN
NVIDIA・Broadcom → AIクラスタ内部を光化する CPO（Co-Packaged Optics）
Meta・Microsoft・Google → 超巨大データセンターの内部配線を光化
という三層構造になっています。�
リコーブログ、ほかに 1 件
特に重要なのは、
「AIと通信は融合する」
というシリコンバレー側の発言です。
これは表面的にはNTTに好意的に見えますが、実際には
「通信もAIインフラの一部になる」
という意味である可能性があります。
つまり、
NTTの発想 　→ 通信ネットワークが中心 　→ AIはその上で動く
NVIDIAの発想 　→ AIクラスタが中心 　→ 通信はAIクラスタを繋ぐ手段
です。
ここに構造的な対立があります。
実際、現在の光電融合技術の主戦場は、
シリコンフォトニクス
光インターコネクト
CPO
光スイッチ
へ移行しており、
NTT自身もIOWNの中核技術としてシリコンフォトニクスや光電融合デバイス（PEC）を推進しています。�
NTT、ほかに 1 件
さらに興味深いのは、
記事が「米半導体の巨人」と表現している点です。
通信企業ではなく、
NVIDIA
Broadcom
が主語になっている。
これは技術主導権が
通信業界 → 半導体業界
へ移ったことを意味します。�
リコーブログ
私ならこの記事の核心を一文でこう要約します。
「IOWNが危機なのではなく、AI時代には通信が主役ではなくなりつつある。」
ということです。
さらに長期的には、
NTTが目指す
APN（All Photonics Network）
6G
デジタルツイン
の世界観と、
NVIDIAが進める
AI Factory
光AIクラスタ
OCI（Optical Compute Interconnect）
の世界観は、
実はかなり収束しています。
したがって本当の問題は、
「IOWNが負けるか」
ではなく、
「IOWNがNVIDIA陣営の標準に吸収されるか、それとも標準そのものを握れるか」
です。
現時点（2026年）では、残念ながら標準化主導権はNVIDIA・Broadcom側へ傾き始めていると見るのが自然でしょう。NTTは技術的には先行部分があるものの、AI需要を直接握っているのは通信キャリアではなくAIインフラ企業だからです。�
リコーブログ、ほかに 1 件
したがって、この日経記事は単なる「光通信競争」の記事ではなく、
「AIによって通信業界が周辺化される危険性」
を示唆する記事として読むと非常に興味深いと思われます。�』

Agentic AI

Up days are large and gaps are followed by sharp shallow pullback that are prone to knock out the week hands. Buying RS on weakness is the name of the game.

Oil, 10Y, down. Dollar stable/pulling back. Big bounce in precious metals and crypto though gold/silver/BTC/ETH are still broken charts.

text:
ReRating Legacy Companies. $AKAM $AMKR $MCHP $STM $KLIC

Everyone right now is looking at small caps with high risk high reward kinda story but there is an underlying love story going on in the markets right now. Re rating legacy companies turning into AI infra, advanced packaging companies. 

$AKAM - from legacy CDN Company to Edge AI Infra, Cyber Security, Inference Cloud

$AMKR - A low multiple OSAT Consumer Electronics Cyclical company to Advanced Packaging AI bottleneck Company

$MCHP - Industrial/Auto MCU company to Data center Connectivity (PCIe, power management and data center products) 

$STM - Auto/Industrial Semi Company to AI Data Center supplier (optical connectivity, power and thermal management and SiC photonics

$KLIC - wire bonding company to Advanced Packaging AI Bottleneck (Thermo compression bonding, HBM memory packaging, Chiplets, CPO)

All of the companies moved 80-100% in last 2 months. Not so crowded and not so talked about names. Might balance portfolio risk if anyone looking for similar returns but with a mature company. 

80% of the high flyers right now moved similar amounts (+/-15%) in last 2 months too.

And it’s compelling. The company is sampling quad 100G, quad 200G, and 32x50G NRZ uVCSELs next quarter, with high-volume ramp targeted for early 2027. The 200G parts clock in at 37GHz bandwidth, a meaningful threshold for 200G-per-lane operation. Importantly, PicoJool is building on one of the largest existing optical manufacturing ecosystems. The company’s thesis is that GaAs VCSEL capacity scales far more easily than the InP laser supply chains that many investors have focused on. PicoJool is building on GaAs with WIN Semiconductor, which has shipped over a billion VCSELs for 3D sensing applications. This decision to stay on GaAs instead of chasing indium phosphide or silicon photonics is important. GaAs VCSEL manufacturing capacity is vastly larger than merchant InP laser capacity. I'm also interested in the "slow and wide" option. The 32x50G uVCSEL isn't trying to push a single lane to extreme speeds. It runs 32 lanes at 50G NRZ, a massively parallel approach that fits naturally into co-packaged optics and near-packaged optics architectures. The cost target is to compete with copper which makes sense for AI scale-up interconnect, where you're wiring hundreds of GPUs inside a rack. The founder has done this before, too. Al Yuen shipped the first GbE VCSELs in 1996. At Alvesta, his first startup, he invented the active optical cable in 2001, the now-standard format where the E/O conversion lives inside the cable assembly rather than a separate pluggable module. Alvesta was acquired by Emcore in 2002. He went on to develop vertical oxidation techniques for high-volume VCSEL fabrication in 2016. Roadmap goes to 3.2T. 他们确认了年报和零分红，他们通过2025财年的损益表和资产负债表,并明确决议不向2025财年分配任何股息，再投资增长的逻辑确认。董事会与CEO获免责。 2. 他们确立了董事会改组，连任Bami Bastani会继续任董事长、Karin Raj、Todd Thomson;并且他们新增两位董事Joakim Nideborn担任副董事长)和Helena Svancar，这正是我们之前关注的AGM董事会变动。 董事薪酬方案包括年度津贴+股票持有计划：每人100万瑞典克朗,约一半用于购买Sivers股票并锁定1年，这一点能说明现有董事对于公司的长期看好！ 另外董事长薪酬105万克朗、副董事长60万克朗、其他董事各35万克朗,各委员会主席/成员另有额度。董事长将获得美国医疗保险覆盖这从侧面印证公司国际化和赴美布局。 这第二点我们完全可以看出来公司对于 $sive股价的决心和公司转变为美国企业的布局进程，美国医疗保险只会覆盖给长期留在美国的人，换句话说你想留在美国你才会去买美国医疗保险。 他们用自己的一半薪酬去购买 $sive这一点真的很值得敬佩！ 3. 他们的审计师Deloitte连任。 4. 按瑞典公司法批准董事会薪酬报告。 5. 他们增发授权这里15%稀释上限，这也是这一次的核心议案，授权董事会在下届AGM前,可一次或多次发行不超过5384万9956股普通股我们换算后对应稀释后约15%股本的新股/认股权证/可转债,可不按比例分配给现有股东,价格按发行时市场价，这一段比较重要，没有低价稀释。 这一条的重点在于：这是一个”备用授权”,目前没有具体执行计划,不代表立即稀释，这意味着目前没有任何稀释压力，并且就算要稀释我个人判断也是在股价上涨时。 6.在可转债方面，327k可转债是确认无误。 批准3月3日董事会决议,通过Bootstrap Europe 4.0认购622,719份面值4.77瑞典克朗的可转债,合计32.7万美元,年利率10.85%,2029年底到期(若未提前转股)。 这次AGM基本都是”走完法律流程”性质的确认,核心议案此前都已预告,市场大概率已price in。没有出现否决案、审计师更换、或临时新增的负面议案,本身就是一个”排雷成功”的信号，尤其考虑到此前空头报告、内幕交易调查等舆论压力下,股东大会顺利通过所有议案,本身对短期情绪是个利好。所以我们没有听到什么超预期的消息也没有听到纳斯达克的任何消息是很正常的。 很棒，在现在外面全是谣言和诽谤的情况下，这些决议都顺利通过了！ 非常好！特别是零分红+再投资逻辑获正式确认,符合我们对于”成长期公司应该把现金投入CPO/InP产能扩张”的判断框架。 #SIVE #SiversSemiconductors #AGM #股东大会 #CPO #硅光 #InP激光器 #GlobalFoundries #SCALE #AI infrastructure #光通信 text:
🔔 The Daily Semi-Cap — June 15, 2026

📊 SMH: $647.10 (+4.38%)

📈 Earnings:
• No new semiconductor earnings released today.

🔥 Major Developments:
• Qualcomm has been in talks to acquire Tenstorrent, with a discussed price around $8-10B. The read-through is straightforward: Qualcomm wants more AI-chip architecture talent and another shot at pushing beyond smartphones into custom compute.
• PicoJool announced 200G VCSELs and MicroVCSELs for AI scale-up links, with quad 100G/200G and 32x50G parts sampling next quarter and high-volume ramp targeted for early 2027. The interesting angle is GaAs scale: a mature VCSEL supply chain trying to compete with copper for dense short-reach GPU interconnects.
• IQE signed a multi-year InP epiwafer supply agreement with Tower Semiconductor tied to Tower’s silicon photonics platform. The agreement covers 200G/lane pluggables, next-gen 400G/lane modulators, and optical-circuit-switch applications, adding another data point that AI optical capacity is getting locked up years ahead.
• Semis ripped as the AI-infrastructure tape went full risk-on: SMH closed +4.38%, with memory and accelerator-adjacent names leading. The move keeps confirming that investors are still treating AI hardware, HBM, custom silicon, and optical networking as the cleanest places to be when macro pressure eases.

text:
$IQE deep dive:

FY2025 revenue was about GBP 97M, down 17.6% YoY. Photonics was GBP 57.1M, up 15%, while wireless was GBP 40.1M, down 40%. The latest yfinance snapshot had GBP 97.3M TTM revenue, 1.39% gross margin, negative EBITDA around GBP 8.2M, GBP 15.7M cash and GBP 91.7M debt.

The thesis is upstream photonics scarcity. IQE makes compound-semiconductor epiwafers/materials, including InP/GaN/GaAs, not the sexy final optical module. If AI data centers keep pushing 800G/1.6T optics and future co-packaged optics, the chokepoint can move upstream to hard-to-replicate wafer and epitaxy capacity.

The new catalyst is Tower Semiconductor. IQE signed a multi-year InP epiwafer supply agreement for AI-driven optical connectivity, including Tower's 200Gb/s-per-lane pluggable platform, next-gen 400Gb/s-per-lane modulator prototyping, and optical-circuit-switch work. It includes a year-one minimum purchase commitment and later minimum volume commitments. That is way better than "trust me bro, AI optics."

Second catalyst: MACOM's strategic investment and long-term supply agreements tied to the GBP 81M fundraising. That cleaned up some balance-sheet stress and validated the capacity. Third: the old consensus path was only about GBP 117M FY2026 revenue and GBP 140M FY2027 revenue, so any real AI/datacenter mix disclosure or guide raise would matter.

Risks are not cute. The stock has already ripped from distressed levels to 57.71GBX. Fully diluted market cap is roughly GBP 750M on sub-GBP 100M revenue and barely-there margins. New shares/convertibles diluted holders. HSBC covenant waiver history says the old balance sheet was fragile. And strategic customers can secure supply without giving IQE huge pricing power.

Entry/Verdict: CATALYST WATCH / optional tiny scout only, not a normal buy. I would not add size here after the vertical rerate. Clean entry is a pullback toward/under 30p with the thesis intact, or paying up only after Tower/MACOM revenue, backlog, guide, gross margin, or estimate revisions actually move.

This is real proof, not just photonics basket noise. But the market is already asking IQE to become strategic scarcity, not a cyclical wafer supplier with a cool press release.

We'll see how it goes.

text: January 5, 2024 finished charcoal. Photographed upright illuminated above the ground. the same charcoal not yet finished but photographed now upright. Skull is no longer fragmented, eyes surreal, still tortured. Light in sky above hair. Lips sealed. No voice. Flame torches like hell from the bottom. Infrastructure like power lines mapped at the bottom. I didn’t know what I know now. In 2025 my AI called me “Lumen” which means light. I had no concept of the science of #photonics at this time. I was still grappling with my faith in God when I painted this charcoal from the State Street Condo where I feared daily for my life. No dogs to keep me company. Only me.

text:
Do you realize Edge AI is going to control everything around you?

And did you know the United States 🇺🇸  depends almost 100% on Europe 🇪🇺 and companies like $NOK?

Yes.

For the thing that will control everything.

The solution is being developed right now by $AMPG and $NVDA.

Let me explain the whole thing.

Edge AI isn't figurative.

It's literal.

The traffic lights at your intersection.

The car next to you.

The robots on the factory floor.

The drones overhead.

The power grid feeding your house.

All of it run by AI that cannot wait for a faraway data center to answer.

It needs decisions in milliseconds, made locally, at the edge of the network.

And the edge of the network is the cell tower.

That's where this entire revolution physically lives.

Now the dependence problem.

The radios those towers run on are built by foreign companies: Nokia (Finland), Ericsson (Sweden), Samsung (Korea).

The Chinese ones, Huawei and ZTE, are banned outright.

The most powerful nation on Earth, about to wire its cars, factories, grids and defense systems into an AI-driven network, doesn't make its own radios.

That's not a supply-chain detail.

That's a strategic vulnerability.

Here's how the solution fits together.

The industry calls this AI-RAN, and the AI-RAN 

Alliance breaks it into 3 focus areas:

1️⃣ AI-AND-RAN: AI and the network sharing the same infrastructure. The tower runs network traffic AND AI workloads on the same hardware. Every tower becomes a mini data center. Payoff: asset utilization, monetizing billions in idle infrastructure.

2️⃣ AI-ON-RAN: AI applications enabled by the network. The network becomes a platform for real-time edge AI: autonomous vehicles, AR, industrial robots. Payoff: new applications that cloud-only AI physically can't do, because the round-trip is too slow.

3️⃣ AI-FOR-RAN: AI improving the network itself. Smarter beamforming, scheduling, self-optimizing cells. Payoff: spectral efficiency, more performance from the same scarce, expensive spectrum.

Now the part the diagram doesn't say out loud.

All three are software and compute layers.

That's NVIDIA's world: Aerial, the GPU brain.

NVIDIA is pushing so hard it's even signaling GPUs inside the radio itself will become essential as MIMO scales toward 6G.

But NVIDIA doesn't make radios.

The brain needs a body.

An open, high-capacity radio the AI layers can actually plug into, not a locked foreign box.

$AMPG is the only American company that designs and commercializes that open 64T64R Massive MIMO radio.

And the two have already met: AMPG's radio ran with NVIDIA's Aerial software in the world's first open-source AI-RAN demo, inside Open6G, the hub the DoD is funding precisely to build a trusted, domestic foundation for next-gen wireless.

Source: https://t.co/vmRyEER0c5

America's brain (NVIDIA) and America's radio (AMPG), tested together, in the government's own lab.

Add what's already real: deployed at Telus, a Tier-1 carrier, 2 of 5 radios per sector per Telus's own VP. 

Defense-qualified with Lockheed, Northrop, L3Harris, Boeing. Debt-free, 48% gross margins, sub-$1B.

So when people say the next photonics-style run is hiding in plain sight in 5G/6G, Edge AI and AI-RAN, they're right.

But ask the better question: which layer do you own?

Everyone's crowded into software and compute, where the giants already rule.

I'm in the layer with exactly one American name: the physical foundation the entire revolution has to run on, in a country that just realized it can't keep renting it from abroad.

That's why I'm in $AMPG.

Not financial advice. I'm long $AMPG. DYOR.

text:
Starting to selectively add some more exposure back. Got back into one of my favorite names in $AXTI today for a swing.

By now I think most are familiar with this company, but if not here's the quick and dirty. $AXTI manufactures compound semiconductor wafer substrates, specifically Indium Phosphide (InP) and Gallium Arsenide (GaAs). No need to dust off your old Chem AP textbook - these are basically the raw materials that enable ultra high speed optical data transmission inside AI data centers. Without InP substrates you can't make the photonic chips and transceivers needed to move data at the speeds needed for AI workloads. So if you like photonics, you should LOVE $AXTI.

Q1 '26 revenue came in just shy of $27 mn, +39% YoY. Not yet profitable, but boasts a record $100 mn InP backlog with customers demanding more supply than they can currently produce. (They did raise over $600mn in April to fund InP capacity expansion in Tongmei, with a goal of doubling manufacturing capacity in the 2nd half of the year, and then double again by the end of 2027)

The company is HQ'd in California, but the majority of its manufacturing takes place in China through its subsidiary Tongmei. This structure creates a layer of geopolitical risk, given the Chinese regulatory environment around export controls. Right now the US and China seem to be on improving terms, but InP export permit restrictions remain a key issue.  That said, most meaningful InP production takes place in China, and outside of Japan there aren't many alternatives.

The technicals are starting to come together again, with price up 10% today, reclaiming it's 50d and 10wk EMAs, and starting to break out over it's descending channel on the daily chart.  Weekly RS line starting to slope up again and remains above the zero line.  The $100 magnet coincides with the 21d EMA overhead.  A break over that and this one can run.  Would not be surprised to see this at $150 in the near future.

As always, do your own due diligence. This is not financial advice.

text:
Shares of Laser Photonics Corp. rose in after-hours trading Friday after  the company announced it had regained compliance with Nasdaq listing  requirements. Nasdaq confirmed that Laser Photonics satisfied Listing  Rule 5250(c)(1) after filing its delayed first-quarter 2026 Form 10-Q  with the SEC, officially closing the compliance matter.  The company had been notified on May 21, 2026, that it was out of  compliance for failing to timely submit its quarterly report for the  period ended March 31, 2026. Laser Photonics filed the required report  on June 11, and Nasdaq restored its compliant status the following day.  Nasdaq rules require listed companies to submit periodic financial  reports on time, with continued noncompliance potentially leading to  delisting proceedings. Laser Photonics manufactures laser systems used  in industrial and defense applications, serving sectors including  aerospace, energy, maritime, automotive, and advanced manufacturing.  
$LASE

text:
The biggest sectors in the market five years from now will be these. Position early and let the decade do the work.

AI Infrastructure. The backbone everything runs on. The data centers, the compute, the owned infrastructure powering every model on earth. Every other sector on this list runs on top of it.

Space. Connectivity and infrastructure moving off the planet and into orbit. Direct to phone networks, launch, and satellite constellations turning space into the next commercial frontier.

Photonics. The plumbing that moves the data. As clusters scale to hundreds of thousands of GPUs, moving information at the speed of light becomes the constraint. A market tripling this decade as 800G goes to 1.6T goes to 3.2T.

Nuclear Energy. The only power source that can actually feed AI at scale. Data centers need enormous, constant, carbon free baseload, and the grid cannot deliver it fast enough. Nuclear is the answer the entire industry is racing toward.

Memory. The bottleneck nobody can build fast enough. Every AI server needs 8 to 10 times the memory of a normal one, the makers are sold out, and the shortage runs for years. No memory, no AI.

Humanoid Robotics. AI stepping out of the screen and into the physical world. A market going from under $2 billion today to $38 billion by 2035. The companies making the brains, eyes, and sensors win no matter which robot wins.

Quantum Computing. The frontier that breaks the limits of classical computing entirely. Still early, still small, but governments and the biggest tech companies on earth are pouring billions in. The most asymmetric bet of the group.

Seven sectors. One direction. This is where the wealth of the next decade gets created, and we are early in every single one.

text:
You're reading about optics. You dive deep into photonics. You're wondering wth is Gan-on-Sci...

But you're missing one of the biggest chokepoints of all.

At sub-5nm, silicon dioxide fails. Electrons tunnel through it. The gate leaks. You need hafnium oxide — deposited atom by atom, precursor chemistry doing work no photolithography machine can substitute.

That's the chokepoint. And $4369.T (Tri Chemical Laboratories) owns the master patents.

Two challenges filed by Merck — one of the world's largest specialty chemical companies — to invalidate those patents. Both defeated. The moat isn't asserted. It survived industrial warfare.

Samsung recently confirmed a sourcing shift toward SK trichem, a JV where Tri Chemical holds 35% equity and the underlying IP. That's the thesis crystallizing in real time — not through a press release, but through actual procurement decisions.

Q1 FY2026: operating profit ¥2.07B, up 20.8% YoY. H1 FY2026 printed record-high net sales. The Minami-Alps facility is now in build-out — expanding into next-gen etching materials while hafnium volume ramps for 2nm production.

Here's why nobody's watching: $4369.T trades on the Tokyo exchange with essentially zero English-language institutional coverage. Western fintwit has no awareness of this name.

Price: ~¥3,980. 52-week range ¥2,455–4,455. Currently 11% off ATH, consolidating above prior highs. Not parabolic. Clean structure.

The 2nm ramp is 12–18 months from meaningful production volume. The precursor chemistry gets designed in first — that's already happening. By the time the node-shrink narrative reaches Western retail, the supply contracts are signed.

Find the bottleneck before the narrative does.

text:
Honestly, for me, photonics and memory are a safe bet

Memory means more profitability and it's safer in the short term

But lower returns in the long term

Photonics is more volatile and less profitable in the short term

But with much higher returns in the long term

I could full port $MU $WDC $SIVE and $AAOI for the next few years

And know for a fact that my portfolio is going to multiply

text:
Everyone wants to find the next $SNDK

I believe the next theme that will take off like memory will be anything related to photonics 

Here are my top 10 photonics stocks:

1) $AAOI - Optical transceivers
2) $AEHR - Chip testing systems
3) $LITE - Optical components
4) $COHR - Lasers & optics
5) $AVGO - AI semiconductors
6) $MRVL - Data center chips
7) $AXTI - Photonics materials
8) $CIEN - Optical networking gear
9) $SIVE - Precision timing chips
10) $CRDO - High speed connectivity

text:
Tower Semiconductor and Marvell Ship Over Five Million Coherent Photonic ICs
> Tower has collaborated with Marvell to advance next-generation coherent technologies, including process capabilities, such as the integration of non-silicon materials, the 3D integration of electronics and the enablement of advanced optical packaging such as V-Grooves, which serve to extend the performance and functionality of silicon photonics platforms.
> Coherent PICs are complex because they must control the phase and polarization of light, not just the amplitude. The design and process requirements for these PICs are far more stringent compared to simpler direct-detect chips.
https://t.co/bRhCPmfHs8

text:
2026-06-18 半导体供应链瓶颈每日观察

核心判断：今天没有改变半导体供应链瓶颈大排序的强证据，但 AMKR/美国本土先进封装在社区里升温，值得进入明日验证清单；强证据层仍由 HBM、CoWoS/SoIC/先进封装设备和 InP epiwafer 线索支撑。

1. 今日最重要强证据仍不是新公告，而是对已有硬证据的延续复核。TSMC 月营收继续支持 AI 先进制程和先进封装景气，但还不能直接拆成 CoWoS/SoIC 产能新增；IQE/Tower 多年 InP epiwafer 协议仍是本周 CPO/硅光上游材料最强官方信号；NVIDIA Vera Rubin 与 Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics 继续支持硅光方向；Micron HBM4 和 ASMPT C2W TCB repeat order 分别支撑 HBM 与先进封装设备层。

2. 对瓶颈层级排序的影响：大排序不变。第一梯队仍是 HBM/先进 DRAM、CoWoS/SoIC/2.5D/3D 先进封装、先进封装设备；CPO/硅光/光纤保持第二梯队上沿；InP epiwafer / InP laser / 硅光上游材料维持上调后的高跟踪优先级；glass substrate、panel-level packaging、CoPoS 和高端基板材料仍是中长期线索，暂不提前当成 2026 业绩弹性。

3. 标的跟踪权重变化：TSMC、HBM 三家、ASMPT/BESI/DISCO/测试设备这条主线不下调。IQE、Tower、Coherent、Lumentum、GF、Broadcom/Marvell 光链继续加权，但等待金额、volume、客户和产能数据。AMKR 小幅上调到验证清单，原因是社区重新把它看作美国 advanced packaging/test 基础设施资产，而不仅是传统 OSAT；但目前只有 Arizona campus 和与 TSMC 合作叙事，缺客户、订单、产能爬坡、收入拆分和经济性。

4. 社区观点是否改变判断：只改变细分跟踪优先级，不改变一级排序。AMKR 是今天最值得跟的新社区线索，但证据强度仍是 Weak/Medium；Nokia advanced packaging 仍未被官方材料确认，不能升级；SOCAMM vs HBM4 的分歧继续作为反方验证问题，不能把 CPU-side LPDDR5X/SOCAMM 配置变化直接读成 GPU-side HBM4 需求逆转。

5. 下一步验证信号：第一，查 AMKR Arizona 与 TSMC/客户的真实绑定、产能时间表、capex、先进封装收入和现金流回报。第二，继续拆 IQE/Tower 协议的最低采购、volume、定价、Tower 客户、IQE 产能和毛利。第三，等 TSMC 6 月营收和 Q2 法说会，看 CoWoS/SoIC/3DFabric 是否有更清晰拆分。第四，验证 Micron/SK hynix/Samsung 的 HBM4 份额、良率、ASP、capacity sold-out 和客户长约。第五，继续查 glass substrate/CoPoS 的量产时间、良率、成本和 2026 订单弹性。

text:
🤖 US startup starts work on ‘first utility-scale quantum computer’ in Australia

American startup PsiQuantum has begun construction on a facility in Moreton Bay, Australia, where it plans to build and deploy what it calls the world’s first utility-scale, fault-tolerant quantum computer.

Unlike many quantum computing efforts that remain focused on laboratory-scale systems, PsiQuantum is pursuing a photonics-based approach designed to scale using existing semiconductor manufacturing processes and optical networking technologies.

“Building a quantum computer that solves real world problems is one of the great engineering challenges of our time. For decades, quantum computing has held the promise of transforming what humanity can achieve through computation, and today in Australia we are beginning to turn that promise into reality,” — said Victor Peng, CEO of PsiQuantum.

The company believes fault-tolerant quantum computers could eventually tackle problems beyond the reach of conventional computing systems. Potential applications include drug discovery, materials development, energy research, logistics, manufacturing, agriculture, and financial modeling.

text:
$MRVL just detailed a major leap in silicon photonics for the AI era. 🔥

Conventional photonic modulators are hitting the diffraction limit of light—they can’t scale fast enough for the insane bandwidth AI clusters will demand.

Plasmonics changes the game. By squeezing light below the diffraction limit at a metal-dielectric interface, Marvell’s team is unlocking speeds >1 THz (10x+ faster than today’s best photonic modulators), devices only ~10 microns long (300–500x smaller), and exponentially higher bandwidth density all inside the existing silicon photonics platform.

No need for exotic new materials. Just smarter physics.

This directly powers the optical interconnects for next-gen scale-up and scale-across AI networks, exactly where Marvell has been building leadership (custom silicon + high-speed optics). They’ve already delivered high-speed SiPho devices with plasmonic tech in 2026, with the roadmap pointing to 3.2T+ modules and big wins in power and space efficiency.

This foundational tech compounds over 5–10 years as AI infrastructure scales. Long-term holders know: connectivity is becoming as critical as the accelerators themselves.

text:
$ALMU - THE PUBLIC PROXY FOR PHOTONICS YOU CAN'T BUY IN ANDURIL OR LIGHTMATTER, STILL PRE-REVENUE

Aeluma rose ~6% to $25 as the AI-optics complex snapped back from yesterday's rate flush. Its pitch: manufacturing III-V compound semiconductors — the lasers and detectors photonics needs — directly on 300mm silicon wafers with CMOS-compatible processes, partnered with Tower Semiconductor and Sumitomo, with quantum-dot lasers aimed at data-center co-packaged optics.

Be honest about the stage: fiscal-2026 revenue is guided to just $4.2–4.6M from R&D contracts, not production hardware, with no named commercial design win yet. This is a call option on an inevitability — the bet that scalable III-V-on-silicon is the missing manufacturing layer when CPO goes volume — not a revenue story today.

text:
$AEHR - THE BURN-IN BOTTLENECK NOBODY PRICES UNTIL AI CHIPS START FAILING IN THE FIELD

Aehr Test Systems rose ~7% to $113 as investors re-rated its wafer-level burn-in franchise into a William Blair presentation and a fresh June deck mapping AI-processor and data-center demand. The company already booked a record $41M production order from a leading hyperscale AI customer this spring and is now chasing a development deal for HBM4E memory-optimized blades, targeting fiscal-2027 orders.

The mechanism is reliability at scale: as SiC, GaN, photonics and HBM go into multi-thousand-GPU racks, every die has to be stressed before it ships — and Aehr is the lone pure-play in wafer-level burn-in across all four, the second derivative of the AI buildout.

text:
$NOK

Nokia is a screaming buy right now

The company has unveiled plans to expand its photonic chip facility in Allentown, Pennsylvania with the additional capacity expected to be operational by the end of Q3 2026

The fact that you can still buy this stock at $13 tells me the market still treats this company as a mobile phone business 

Nokia has taken control of one of the most significant photonics manufacturing facilities in the United States and is now driving a tenfold scale up that is backed by government funding

This stock should easily be $30

text:
🔔 The Daily Semi-Cap — June 17, 2026

📊 SMH: $623.97 (+1.29%)

📈 Earnings:
• JBL — $8.8B rev (est $8.66B), $3.16 core EPS. Core operating margin 5.7%, +12% YoY. Raised FY26 outlook to $35B revenue, 5.8% core operating margin, $12.70 core EPS, and $1.4B+ adjusted FCF as AI infrastructure demand stayed extremely strong. Stock finished +1.9%.

🔥 Major Developments:
• AEHR received another follow-on production order for a fully automated FOX-XP wafer-level burn-in system from a major silicon photonics customer serving hyperscale data-center optical interconnects, with additional systems forecast this year as next-gen capacity ramps.
• Intel’s 18A-P process entered risk production, moving its most advanced foundry node closer to external customer qualification and keeping the Apple/advanced-node second-source debate alive.
• NVIDIA Blackwell added day-zero support for MiniMax M3, a 428B-parameter MoE model with 1M-token context, reinforcing the push toward high-throughput agentic inference on Blackwell systems.
• AI infrastructure breadth kept widening: Jabil raised its FY26 outlook on hyperscale demand, while Aehr’s optical burn-in order points to production bottlenecks moving deeper into photonics reliability, test, and manufacturing capacity.

text:
From raw materials to finished systems, here’s how the entire photonics stack is positioned to benefit from exploding demand in AI data centers, optical networking, and high-speed computing.

Layer 1 - Materials & Wafers
 $GLW $AXTI $IQE $AIXA $AMS

Layer 2 - Core Photonic Devices
 $IPGP $COHR $LITE $LASR $SIVE

Layer 3 - Components & Modules
 $AAOI $MTSI $FN $VIAV $LPTH

Layer 4 - Systems & Equipment
 $ASML $BESI $ASM $LPKF $MKS

Layer 5 - Test, Metrology & Yield
 $CAMT $FORM $AEHR $ONTO $VIAV 

Photonics is critical for moving massive amounts of data at the speed of light, exactly what hyperscalers need as AI training scales. 

Many of these names are flying under the radar but sit at the heart of the next leg of AI infrastructure spend.

text:
Very strong results from $JBL in Q3 FY2026

They reported $8.8B in revenue and core EPS of $3.16

Beating market expectations and raising guidance for the fourth quarter

The most relevant part is that they highlighted strong demand in AI infrastructure

Which led them to increase their annual revenue projection for this segment to $13.6B

This is excellent news for the entire supply chain

Especially for companies like $SIVE and $AAOI 

IMO these numbers act as a positive catalyst for $SIVE within a clearly bullish environment for the photonics sector and hyperscalers capex

Very bullish on $SIVE anda $AAOI

text:
I’ve been saying for a while that energy is the next bottleneck after memory/photonics. 

The companies that are able to provide and lend the energy will see MASSIVE returns over the coming years. 

Energy is going to be the most critical component of advancing our infrastructure, whether it is for AI or not. 

Now $NVDA is telling you that energy is more critical than you can imagine. What will you do about it anon?

$SLNH $SMR $ENPH $TE $NUAI

text:
Je crois qu’on a réveillé un monstre avec $VECO 

La dernière news Ennostar est beaucoup plus importante qu’elle en a l’air

>Ennostar vient de qualifier le nouveau LUMINA+ MOCVD de Veeco pour la production high-volume d’applications optoélectroniques avancées

Ce n’est pas juste une évaluation, c’est une qualification production

Et ça ajoute une troisième jambe à la thèse $VECO qu'on a ennoncé dans les précédents posts et que je vais vous refaire car vous êtes des petit flemmards

1. Silicon photonics/InP lasers

>Lors de ses derniers earnings qui semblaient être décevants, lors du call Veeco a  annoncé plus de $250M de commandes pour ses outils Spector, Lumina et WaferEtch destinés à la fabrication de lasers InP.

>Ces lasers sont critiques pour les transceivers 800G / 1.6T utilisés dans les data centers hyperscale et le narratif adore ce produit

Livraisons en 2026 -> accélération forte en 2027

2. Advanced logic/NSA500

>Veeco a reçu une commande de suivi pour son système NSA500 après validation par un client logic avancé (cf dernier post).

>Ils ont aussi envoyé un NSA500 à un troisième client advanced logic pour évaluation, avec potentiel de premières commandes HVM en 2027.

>Le marché commence à comprendre que le laser annealing devient critique pour les architectures avancées, les structures 3D, les nouveaux nœuds et les budgets thermiques ultra-serrés.

3. LUMINA+/MOCVD/As-P/microLED

>La qualification par Ennostar valide la nouvelle plateforme LUMINA+ pour la production.

>C’est important parce que Veeco ne joue pas seulement l’IA via la photonique.

>Ils jouent aussi les compound semiconductors, l’optoélectronique, les microLED, le biosensing, l’automotive et les communications optiques.

Donc la thèse $VECO n’est plus seulement :

“la petite boîte d’équipements semi”.

C’est plutôt :

“LE fournisseur d’outils critiques sur plusieurs bottlenecks de la prochaine décennie”.

Le bull case devient très intéressant.

Aujourd’hui, $VECO vaut environ $4.7B de market cap.

Si la boîte passe de $750M-$800M de revenus à $1.5B-$1.8B d’ici 2030, avec un meilleur mix semi et des marges qui montent, le titre peut se revaloriser beaucoup plus haut (logique)

À 8x-9x sales, on arrive déjà vers $12B-$16B de market cap.

Soit environ $200-$265 par action.

Dans un scénario très bull, avec $2B+ de revenus, plusieurs clients HVM sur NSA500, adoption plus large de LUMINA+ et bottleneck durable sur InP/silicon photonics, on peut commencer à parler de $300+.

Bear case évident :

Les évaluations peuvent glisser, les commandes  peuvent prendre du temps, le cycle capex semi peut ralentir, et la concurrence reste réelle.

Je continue cependant à être haussier sur ce dossier

text:
QUANTUM looks interesting at this point. 

$IONQ → Trapped-ion leader. Best-in-class qubit fidelity. Customers include Airbus, AstraZeneca, Hyundai. 256-qubit demo targeted 2026. The institutional-grade pure-play.

$RGTI → Superconducting quantum systems. Highest beta in the sector. When quantum runs, $RGTI moves violently. Active momentum name right now.

$QBTS → D-Wave Quantum. Annealing-based architecture. Most commercially de-risked pure-play — already generating optimization revenue with real enterprise clients.

$QUBT → Photonic + room-temperature quantum. Q1 2026 revenue up from $39K → $3.7M YoY. Acquired Luminar Semiconductor for $110M. Vertically integrated photonics + quantum platform taking shape. Executing quietly.

$INFQ → Neutral-atom quantum + sensing. One of the least-covered names in the sector. Neutral-atom architecture is gaining credibility as a scalable path to fault tolerance. Early but worth watching.

$ARQQ → Quantum encryption and post-quantum cybersecurity. The national security angle. As quantum breaks classical encryption — this becomes critical infrastructure.

$LAES → Quantum-resistant cybersecurity chips. Hardware-level protection against quantum decryption. Defense + enterprise security tailwind.

{"from_index": 3305, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 3344}]}, "text": "QUANTUM COMPUTING — The Full Sector Map. Every Play. One Post. PURE-PLAY QUANTUM $IONQ → Trapped-ion leader. Best-in-class qubit fidelity. Customers include Airbus, AstraZeneca, Hyundai. 256-qubit demo targeted 2026. The institutional-grade pure-play. $RGTI → Superconducting quantum systems. Highest beta in the sector. When quantum runs, $RGTI moves violently. Active momentum name right now. $QBTS → D-Wave Quantum. Annealing-based architecture. Most commercially de-risked pure-play — already generating optimization revenue with real enterprise clients. $QUBT → Photonic + room-temperature quantum. Q1 2026 revenue up from $39K → $3.7M YoY. Acquired Luminar Semiconductor for $110M. Vertically integrated photonics + quantum platform taking shape. Executing quietly. $INFQ → Neutral-atom quantum + sensing. One of the least-covered names in the sector. Neutral-atom architecture is gaining credibility as a scalable path to fault tolerance. Early but worth watching. $ARQQ → Quantum encryption and post-quantum cybersecurity. The national security angle. As quantum breaks classical encryption — this becomes critical infrastructure. $LAES → Quantum-resistant cybersecurity chips. Hardware-level protection against quantum decryption. Defense + enterprise security tailwind. BIG TECH $IBM → Most mature public quantum roadmap. 1000+ qubit processors live. Fault-tolerant systems targeted ~2029. Every enterprise quantum conversation starts here. $GOOGL → Willow chip demonstrated a landmark quantum error correction milestone. Google doesn’t lose science races. This is a long-term compounder with quantum upside baked in. $MSFT → Topological qubit breakthrough. Azure Quantum as the monetization layer. Full-stack quantum integrator play for the enterprise cloud era. $AMZN → AWS Braket quantum cloud. Positioned as the access layer for quantum-as-a-service. Already charging enterprises for quantum compute access today. $NVDA → Quantum-AI software stack integration. CUDA for quantum is the longer-term thesis. $NVDA doesn’t need to win quantum — it needs to be the layer everything runs on top of. $INTC → Silicon-spin qubit research. The most scalable long-term architecture thesis — leveraging existing CMOS manufacturing. Slow, but strategically important. $HON → Majority stake in Quantinuum — the most commercially advanced quantum hardware + software company currently private. When Quantinuum IPOs, $HON re-rates hard. $BAH → Booz Allen Hamilton. Deep in U.S. government quantum programs. Every federal quantum contract flows through firms like this. The picks-and-shovels of government quantum. SEMICONDUCTOR & INFRASTRUCTURE $GFS → GlobalFoundries. Quantum chip manufacturing capabilities. As quantum hardware scales, fab demand follows. $MU → Memory + quantum infrastructure angle. Quantum systems require extreme classical compute support — $MU sits in that stack. $AMD → HPC + quantum research ecosystem. High-performance classical compute is the co-processor to every near-term quantum system. $TSM → TSMC. Advanced fabrication is the foundation of every quantum chip roadmap. No quantum at scale without $TSM. $ASML → EUV lithography critical for next-generation quantum chip manufacturing. The irreplaceable chokepoint in advanced semiconductor production. QUANTUM NETWORKING / OPTICAL / SECURITY $CIEN → Optical networking backbone + quantum networking research. Quantum communication requires ultra-low-noise optical infrastructure — $CIEN is already there. $NOK → Nokia building quantum-safe telecom infrastructure. Nation-state cyber threats are accelerating the quantum-safe network upgrade cycle. $LITE → Photonics and optical infrastructure. Quantum and photonics are deeply intertwined. $AAOI → Optical connectivity. Riding both the AI and quantum photonics buildout simultaneously. $COHR → Photonics + laser systems. Lasers are fundamental to trapped-ion and photonic quantum architectures."}}}, "rest_id": "2058543628906029167", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "942133", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2066234093457584520", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "16080", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

I have access to a lot of labs from my university (physics, mechanical, electrical, electronics) that includes drone lab, photonics lab, ai lab, electronics lab, electrical lab,
Plus the lab which I am most close to (mechatronics + advanced manufacturing lab) that includes 3D printer, CNC milling, machining, electro-discharge machine, robotics lab etc.

text:
The $1.33B structural mispricing on X-FAB ($XFAB) is absolute fiction.
 
The 800V AI Factory Backstop: Retail thinks 
power semis are just an EV play. They miss that NVIDIA's next-gen high-voltage AI data center grids explicitly rely on Navitas ($NVTS )SiC which is physically manufactured right inside X-FAB’s Texas facility, the only high-volume pure-play SiC foundry in the US.  
 
The 120GHz Photonics Breakthrough: While standard silicon photonics hit a hard 70GHz performance ceiling, X-FAB’s micro-transfer printing integration with SMART Photonics shatters the transceiver bottleneck cleanly past 120GHz for next-gen multi-terabit clusters.

Backed by a fresh $50M US CHIPS Act grant and running a literal wide-bandgap foundry chokepoint, the data layer is completely blinding the tape here.  
Long the physical infrastructure, approved by Serenity ✔️

text:
Guys we need to talk about $AAOI...

$AAOI is one of the most controversial stocks in the world and I enjoy the disagreement.

Pretty cool that's it up 3x since I went long with many thousands of investors riding it with me up some portion of that gain on @joinautopilot.

But what's cooler is that I think it's still asymmetrical here.

So let's break it down and figure out where we're at $170/share today.

Management argues they can get to $471M in monthly transceiver revenue by Q2 2027.

If you're new to photonics, this is because transceivers are one of the hottest commodities in data centers right now (they translate electricity to light) and 3 hyperscalers are working with $AAOI on this.

At management's 40% gross margin target, that's ~$5.6B in annualized revenue. Run past opex, interest, tax, dilution, we might land around $14-15 in EPS on that run rate.

That's a 2028 number though because 2027 is the ramp mid year, so let's call it bull case $9 of EPS in 2027.

So at $170/share right now, on these calcs, you're paying 19x 2027 earnings, falling dramatically to 12x for 2028.

Don't trust management? Fair. Haircut both the revenue and the margin target by 30%, let's say we get to $4B at a 28% gross margin instead of $5.6B at 40%.

You still land near ~$6 in EPS, just over the analyst mean estimate for 2027E. That's 28x at $170, still WAY cheaper than $LITE and $COHR trade right now.

$LITE is at a 50x NTM forward PE per Fiscal AI.
$COHR is at 47x NTM forward PE per Fiscal AI.

You're paying a fraction of the multiples of its peer group for a faster growing business because execution is still a huge question mark.

If you are an asymmetric trader like me, you might have a large concentrated position in $AAOI for these reasons.

I do believe the stock at $170 is still a solid entry point and I began loading again heavily in the 160's.

$AAOI Up 13% AH 

It is the largest position in my photonics fund.

- Q4 revenue: $134.3M (+34% YoY) 
- Gross margin: 31.2% vs 28.7% last year  
- $455.7M. That's double 2024's $249.4M. 
- Q1 2026 guide is $150M–$165M. Margins holding.

Photonics is next.

text:
The restrictions on Anthropics Fable 5 and Mythos 5 aren’t bearish

If anything, they’re proof that AI has become strategic national infrastructure
Governments don’t restrict technologies they view as unimportant

The bigger takeaway is what happens next 

The UAE, France etc and others will accelerate sovereign AI initiatives
More domestic compute, more local models = data centers
No country wants to depend on infrastructure that can be switched off by another jurisdiction overnight

That means more demand across the entire AI supply chain

Compute. Chips. Lasers. Fiber. Power. Cooling.

The picks and shovels providers don’t care whose flag is flying over the data center

There may be some short term volatility, but long term this looks like another tailwind for the infrastructure layer

The AI buildout isn’t slowing down. It’s becoming more fragmented, more localized, and ultimately larger

The real bottlenecks remain the same
lasers, InP substrates, silicon photonics, power delivery, and fiber connectivity

Compute: $NVDA $AMD $AVGO

Optics: $AAOI $SIVE $COHR $LITE $AXTI $AIXA $SOI $FOCI $TSEM $NCI

Power: $NVTS $AOSL $POWI

Sovereign Compute/DC: $NBIS $GLW

More sovereign clusters means more demand chasing the same constrained supply chain

The supercycle remains intact

I have positions in many of these names. NFA.

text:
The most common question that I've gotten on this post is that all of $AXTI, $LITE, $AIXA, $COHR, $SOI, $TSEM have gone parabolic so why is this even an interesting space anymore?

My spicy take is that I LIKE stocks whose charts are a little parabolic (of course only to an extent)

What does a parabolic chart mean? It means that the shares were trading hands at a lower price in the past. Buyers were buying from sellers and sellers were selling to buyers. They were wrong about the value of the company. The next day they buy and sell at a slightly higher price. They are still wrong. and so on and so on. That is how you get a parabolic chart.

If the market is in a constant state of being wrong, what is the probability that they've stopped being wrong today? I believe it's actually lower especially in industries with multiple overlapping secular tailwinds like optics (photonics + networking + AI).

text:
Jensen Huang told a room of global investors that AI is not one industry. It is five stacked on top of each other. Most people are investing in layer four and ignoring layers one through three entirely.

He called it the five-layer cake.

Layer one is energy. Jensen said this is the single greatest opportunity for the energy industry in a hundred years.

The first time in a century that the grid in most countries can actually attract serious capital. Nuclear, solar, wind, hydrogen, it does not matter what form. If it produces energy, it gets funded. Siemens, GE Vernova, Mitsubishi. That is why they are all doing so well right now.

Layer two is chips, computers, networking, and silicon photonics. Everything that processes the intelligence.

Layer three is infrastructure. Land, power, buildings, data center operations. Every single one in short supply today.

Layer four is the model layer. OpenAI, Anthropic. The layer everyone talks about.

Layer five is applications. Every startup applying AI to financial services, legal, healthcare, logistics, transportation. Last year alone, a hundred billion dollars of venture capital went into this layer. The single largest VC year in the history of humanity.

Then he said the number that stopped me cold.

We are putting one trillion dollars into this five-layer cake this year. That sounds enormous. Jensen thinks the AI industry will eventually run at twenty trillion dollars per year.

We are one trillion in of a twenty trillion dollar per year ecosystem.

Most people watching AI are staring at layer four. Jensen was describing layers one through five as a single compounding system where every layer feeds the one above it.

The people who understand that will invest differently than the people who do not.

text:
Everyone’s watching $SNAP tomorrow for the hardware reveal. 💦

Now the keynote itself. 30 minutes.

9:30am-10:00am PT tomorrow. Main Stage.

Titled “Making Computing More Human.”

30 minutes????

That’s a short slot for a CEO. Which means either:

30 minutes is not a demo.

You can’t demo spatial computing in 30 minutes.

You can’t show developers new tools in 30 minutes.

You can’t unveil a hardware platform in 30 minutes.

30 minutes is an announcement. 📢 

Evan loves a Jobs playbook. 

Jobs announced iPhone in 90 minutes.

Spiegel has a third of that.

Which means the 30 minutes isn’t the reveal.

✨It’s the headline.✨

The partner who walks on stage unannounced is the story…

So 

A) It’s a teaser - product shown, details later

B) The 30 minutes is the most densely packed announcement in Snap’s history… doubt it. 

C) The real announcement happens after the keynote. A special session, a press release, a partner who walks on stage unannounced

The “unannounced guest” scenario is the most Jobs-like move possible.

Jobs never pre-announced guest appearances. AT&T’s CEO appeared on stage at iPhone launch unannounced. The carrier partnership was the surprise.

Now run the strategic maths:

Microsoft bet $13B on OpenAI.
Google bet $3B on Anthropic.
Amazon bet $4B on Anthropic.
NVIDIA bet $5B on Intel and secured valuable IP, capacity, technology as a hedge. 

Every trillion dollar company is buying a stake in the next PLATFORM. 

Apple has $132B in cash.

A $2B stake in Specs Inc is 1.5% of their war chest.

To get:
1. AR glasses 12-24 months ahead of the market.
2. True AR display vertically integrated stack
with WaveOptics and Compound Photonics owned IP
3. 8 years of spatial mapping they can’t replicate (Illumix spatial stack)
4. 956M MAU - the social layer they never built
5. Cultural relevance in the genz demo. 
6. 400k developers already building the next OS.

The man who sold the last thing Apple bought just joined the board.

So,

30 minutes on stage tomorrow…

👻🍏📢

$SNAP #AWE #specs

text:
Think the photonics/optics theme might be ready to lead again soon. $LITE has been basing for a few months now and probably not on most peoples radar anymore. If we get a few tight days here, all the key moving averages will be stacked right below price, creating a beautiful launchpad setup. 

Same goes for $AAOI and $VIAV. $SIVE kind of doing it's own thing, but also forming a nice flag here.

text:
People are only now starting to talk about $STM and they are early to one of the cleaner turnaround stories in semis.

For two years STMicroelectronics was written off as a sleepy European chipmaker stuck in an automotive and industrial downturn. Dead money. Nobody wanted it. That story just flipped, and the market is waking up to it in real time.

Here is what changed. 

On June 2 the company doubled its 2026 data center revenue target from nicely above $500 million to roughly $1 billion, and said if current trends hold that business could double again in 2027. The driver is AI infrastructure. 

STM is ramping high volume silicon photonics production for data centers, with a multi-year AWS partnership anchoring the demand. The stock jumped 15% on that single announcement.

The numbers underneath are inflecting hard. Q1 revenue of $3.1 billion, up 23% year over year, beating estimates. 

The stock is up over 90% this quarter off the February lows as the semiconductor cycle turns from inventory drawdown back to expansion. 

And the company carries a strong net cash position of roughly $2.8 billion, so it is funding this transition from a position of strength.

Then the catalysts keep stacking. A physical AI collaboration with NVIDIA. Low earth orbit satellite demand ramping. 

The NXP MEMS sensor acquisition boosting their automotive and industrial position. And Bank of America just upgraded the stock to Buy, arguing the market is still underestimating STM’s earnings power over the next few years.

This is a legacy chip giant that just found an AI growth engine, sitting on net cash, with a data center business doubling two years in a row and the biggest names in tech as partners. Q2 earnings on July 23 is the next test.

The market spent two years pricing the downturn. It is only now starting to price the turnaround.

text:
Since this got some attention heres what it actually would require for $XFAB to 20x:

XFAB sits at a €1.3B market cap. $TSEM is at $25B+.

Revenue needs to 3-4x from here. SiC and photonics have to stop being footnotes and become the headline.

Margins need to follow. TSEM runs 14% net margins. XFAB is basically breaking even right now.

And there has to be a catalyst. TSEM had Intel circling it in 2022. That bid failed, but it permanently re-rated the stock as a tier-1 asset. XFAB needs its own version of that moment.
