Here is my thesis: 

This is a well established business founded in 1966. 

Their products and solutions include: filtration products for gases & fluids, liquid systems, gas delivery systems, specialty chemistries for deposition and cleaning, wafer carriers and shippers, specialty coatings, premium graphite and silicon carbide, and shippers and trays for protecting and transporting disk drive components, and CMP slurries.

Basically a one stop shop for semiconductor companies, with ~94% of revenue related to semiconductor.

They report two segments: Materials Solutions (MS) and Advanced Purity Solutions (APS), but in the financial report for their 10-Q they break it down by:

- Semiconductor (fabs, equipment and engineering, chemical and materials, semi distributor and other)

- Non-semi

Think of MS as materials only and APS as filtration products.

The company controls roughly 35% to 40% of the global market for microcontamination control (liquid and gas filtration) and holds top-tier share positions in CMP (Chemical Mechanical Planarization) slurries, pads, and specialized wafer carriers. 

Additionally, Entegris and Pall Corporation (a subsidiary of Danaher) are the dominant duopoly leaders, controlling over 60% of the electronic filtration and purification market combined. Entegris specifically holds about 28% of the semiconductor liquid filter market. 

Why does this matter? 

As microchips continue to shrink, transitioning from 14nm nodes to 1.4nm, the margin for error shrinks alongside them. In fact, the allowable defect tolerance has plummeted tenfold, from 7nm to a mere 0.7nm. At this microscopic scale, even the slightest solid, liquid, or gaseous contamination can result in catastrophic financial damage. Entegris estimates that a mere 1% drop in production yield at the 1.4nm stage can drain up to $800 million annually from a fabrication facility. 

The company works hand-in-hand with its clients to design its purification systems directly into the end-to-end manufacturing systems. Because advanced chipmaking is so incredibly detailed, swapping out an established Entegris system for a rival product on an active production line is practically unfeasible which makes Entegris a long term success as long as chips keep getting more advanced. 

Per Q1'26: 

MS sales grew 2.8% YoY while APS sales grew 6.8% YoY. 

MS sales are ~43% of revenue; APS is ~57% 

They did suffer from sales decrease QoQ but that is mostly seasonality based (not a big deal).

Growth geographically is mixed for the most part, while sales of MS and APS are declining in North America, sales of APS significantly down in Japan...they are compensating this by growth in Taiwan for APS and MS.

The company's gross margin and operating margin have both increased YoY. 

The company is expected to finish FY26 at $3.63 EPS, a 134% increase YoY due to stronger AI demand. For FY27, it is estimated to be $4.65, dropping P/E from ~70x to 28x. 

Additional info:

They are also a confirmed supplier to SK Hynix for molybdenum NAND deposition. They supply Molybdenum Dichloride Dioxide (MoO₂Cl₂) which has seen adoption increase because it is cost effective, reduces resistivity in narrow features, and has an advantage over copper for interconnections by not needing a barrier layer.

Tungsten starts failing as NAND starts stacking above 300 layers, which is why molybdenum is seeing adoption.

Customers include:

Micron $MU
Intel $INTC
SK Hynix $000660.KS
Samsung $005930.KS
TSMC $TSM 
ASML $ASML 
Lam Research $LRCX
Tokyo Electron $803.5T
Applied Materials $AMAT

You can argue it is expensive right now, but there is nobody else that can benefit in this space like them and it is not a foreign company."}}}, "previous_counts": {"bookmark_count": 0, "favorite_count": 0, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0}, "rest_id": "2065108957933310219", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "10298", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2067683962353225827", "source": "<a href="http://twitter.com/download/android" rel="nofollow">Twitter for Android</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2191", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Reality check:

• Fabrication paper, not a quantum result. No single-photon purity, no spin coherence, no entanglement.
• 82.9% is optical-coupling yield, not qubit yield.
• Coupling efficiency stays qualitative ("guided emission observed"), no dB number.
• Room temp, 6 nm lines, far from operating conditions.

But zoom out:

Manufacturability is exactly the unglamorous wall that has kept color-center quantum stuck at hero-device demos. Wafer-scale lithography at 82.9% yield is a real step out of that trap.

Direction: the diamond-photonics scaling story is shifting from "can we make one" to "can we make hundreds."

Where this sits in the photonics-for-quantum arc:

• Caltech LEO comb, Cisco quantum switch, Harvard/NIST Yb-in-alumina
• Miraex/EPFL lithium-tantalate transduction
• This paper, diamond emitters lithographically married to alumina

The theme keeps compounding: integrated photonics is the picks-and-shovels layer under every modality.

text:
.@Fujitsu_Global just put 216 diamond quantum-emitter devices on one chip.

82.9% of them lit up. Over 100 waveguide-coupled emitters, all from wafer-scale lithography.

No pick-and-place. The boring bottleneck of diamond photonics just got less boring. 🧵 https://t.co/J3JE5zn8mG

A lot of the AI infrastructure thesis from the past few weeks are starting to play out.

Neocloud / Sovereign AI:
$CRWV 100 → 121
$NBIS 228 → 297

Memory:
Still holding dram long from ~68, added more $SKHYNIX
$MU earnings next week could be another important read-through for HBM demand. (Jun 24)

Photonics:

China just approved the first major 2026 InP substrate export batch, providing some near-term relief to one of the optical bottlenecks discussed in my photonics thread.

I’m still watching this closely. Demand for 800G/1.6T AI interconnects continues to explode, so a single export batch doesn’t solve the structural shortage, it simply unlocks some capacity. The real question is whether supply can keep up with AI cluster buildouts over the next 12–24 months.

Meanwhile:
• HBM4E sampling next month
• SK Hynix US ADR listing
• SK Hynix earnings (Jul 23)
• Ongoing sovereign AI buildouts globally

The AI trade keeps moving up the stack.

First GPUs.
Then neoclouds.
Then photonics.
Now memory.

The biggest winners continue to come from identifying the bottlenecks before the market fully prices them in.

🇺🇸 | نتائج توصيات السوق الأمريكي اليوم

يوم جديد يؤكد أن قوة التداول ليست في كثرة الصفقات، بل في دقة الاختيار وإدارة الخروج.

تم اليوم إغلاق صفقتين على أرباح قوية:

✅سهم Laser Photonics LASE
من 2.38 إلى 2.65
ربح محقق: +11.3%

✅سهم Roblox RBLX
من 43 إلى 49.13
ربح محقق: +14.3%

وفي المقابل، تم إغلاق صفقة ResMed RMD عند وقف الخسارة بخسارة محدودة تقارب -2%، وهذا جزء طبيعي من أي خطة تداول منضبطة.

📍الخلاصة:
نترك الأرباح تكبر عندما تسير الصفقة في صالحنا، ونغلق الخسارة مبكرًا عندما لا تؤكد الحركة توقعاتنا.

💥هكذا تُبنى النتائج:
ربح قوي عند تحقق الأهداف، وخسارة محدودة عند فشل السيناريو.

 📍في Stock Basket، نعتمد على الدمج بين إدارة الصفقة باحتراف، والبوت الذكي الذي يساعدنا في فرز وتصنيف الفرص داخل السوق قبل التنفيذ.

👈 تفضل واحجز مكانك لمتابعة صفقات واضحة بمستويات دخول، أهداف، حماية ربح، وإدارة دقيقة أثناء الجلسة، 
-الانضمام للمجموعة الذهبية هو الخطوة الأقرب لك.

📩 للاشتراك والتفاصيل، راسلنا الآن.

⚠️ إخلاء مسؤولية:
جميع المعلومات لأغراض إعلامية فقط وليست توصية مباشرة. يُرجى الرجوع إلى مستشار مالي مرخّص قبل أي قرار.

text:
Not claiming $SIVE builds the plasmonic modulator itself.

The point is that plasmonics does not remove the need for high-performance laser sources it makes the optical input layer even more critical.

That is exactly where Sivers is positioned: high-power DFB laser arrays, external light sources and silicon-photonics integration for AI datacenters / CPO.

And this is now supported by the GlobalFoundries $GFS collaboration, where Sivers’ laser arrays are being integrated into reference designs on GF’s silicon photonics platform, including SCALE for next-gen optical sub-assemblies.

So yes from a system architecture perspective, this is highly relevant to $SIVE

text:
It used to take a carefully controlled lab setup to shape light with precision. Now, that same level of control is moving into real-world systems. 

Spatial light modulators, or SLMs, are evolving to be faster, more stable, and ready for deployment—helping engineers fine-tune light for everything from advanced imaging to quantum applications. 

Hamamatsu’s Brandon Gorakhnauth shares his insights on how what was once experimental is becoming essential and where photonics is headed next. 🔗 https://t.co/jdRyuOvn4e 

#Photonics #Optics #SLM #Engineering #Innovation

text:
Contemporary silicon photonics modulators are constrained by the diffraction limit of light, restricting their ability to scale efficiently to the bandwidths AI infrastructure will require. Plasmonics offers a path forward.

By compressing light below the diffraction limit at a metal-dielectric interface, plasmonic modulators can operate at speeds exceeding 1 THz, more than 10x faster than conventional photonic modulators, while measuring approximately 10 microns in length, 300x to 500x shorter. The result is exponentially higher bandwidth density within the same silicon photonics platform, without requiring a shift to new material systems.

Marvell Senior Director Claudia Hoessbacher and Director Wolfgang Heni of Optical Engineering detail the technology, its applications across scale-up and scale-across networks, and where development stands today.

Read more: https://t.co/3z9yPJxzac

VIGO PHOTONICS SPÓŁKA AKCYJNA (WIG80-#VGO)----19:57----

VIGO Photonics planuje emisję do 131.219 akcji serii G i możliwą sprzedaż do 124.800 akcji przez głównego akcjonariusza

Potencjalny wpływ: Istotny

This is one of those announcements that’s easy to overlook but could become much bigger if it plays out.

NUBURU isn’t just talking about lasers anymore. It’s positioning its blue-laser technology for underwater power infrastructure through the DEEP LIGHT project, which is backed by the Italian Navy. That’s an interesting combination of photonics, defense, and critical infrastructure.

The key thing to remember is that this is still subject to final agreements and technical validation, so it’s not guaranteed revenue yet. But if the project moves into commercialization, it could open the door to a much larger market than many investors are currently pricing in.

Definitely one to keep on the watchlist. $BURU

We are particularly honored by the involvement of General Pietro Serino, former Chief of Staff of the Italian Army, whose distinguished military career spans decades of leadership, strategic planning, international defense cooperation, and operational command at the highest levels.

General Serino’s experience and insight bring significant value as we continue advancing our defense and security strategy, including the planned acquisition of Tekne and the expansion of our broader ecosystem across defense mobility, electronic warfare, critical infrastructure protection, operational resilience, and next-generation directed-energy technologies.

The participation of leaders with proven military and defense expertise reinforces our belief that successful execution requires not only innovative technologies, but also strategic guidance from individuals who understand the evolving needs of modern defense organizations.

As we continue executing our vision, we remain focused on building a platform positioned to support innovation, strengthen strategic partnerships, and create sustainable long-term value for our shareholders.

We thank our shareholders for their continued support and confidence as we advance this important next chapter.

text:
I’m long $NOK

$NOK not being up at least 30% on this news just lets me know most people are missing the turnaround story, the geopolitical angle and the bet on America.

Nokia is 10x-ing photonic chip packaging capacity at its Allentown, Pennsylvania facility. Commercially available by end of Q3.

The numbers confirmed from the June 16 release:

-- ~$30M Nokia capex, plus ~$4M Pennsylvania state aid and ~$10M federal CHIPS investment tax credit
-- 500+ new jobs, nearly doubling the PA workforce
-- $500M+ projected economic impact over 5 years
-- Part of Nokia's broader $4B US R&D and manufacturing commitment

Less than 2% of global semiconductor advanced test and packaging happens in the US. Nokia's Allentown plant is one of the only domestic sites packaging photonic chips into optical modules for AI data centers. Their optical tech can cut energy usage by as much as 75%.

This facility came from Infinera. Nokia acquired Infinera in February 2025. 

Allentown traces directly back to Infinera's decades of work in photonic integrated circuits, coherent optical technology, and domestic optical manufacturing. 

When people look at $NOK and see a sleepy European telecom equipment maker, they are missing that Nokia absorbed one of the most important US-based photonics manufacturing assets in existence and is now scaling it 10x with government money behind it.

The market is still pricing the legacy telecom company. It is not pricing the domestic photonics infrastructure play that Nokia built through the Infinera deal.

Reshoring is its own thesis now, and it has government money and a Q3 deadline attached to it. 

When less than 2% of global ATP happens domestically and Washington wants that number to climb, the few companies that already own US photonics packaging capacity become strategically valuable in a way that has nothing to do with quarterly telecom revenue.

It is a $20B+ telecom giant and the Allentown expansion is a small piece of the total business. 

For a name the market still prices as legacy telecom, getting a domestic photonics packaging franchise with CHIPS Act backing essentially for free inside the valuation is the kind of mispricing I like.

The market is misreading this headline. I rather trade the supply chain.

Bullish AF $NOK

text:
Integrated optical chaos for machine learning acceleration

Many ML and optimization methods need randomness, exploration or probabilistic sampling. In reinforcement learning, stochastic search, Hopfield optimization and probabilistic neural networks, the quality and speed of the entropy source can become part of the computing system itself.

Zhouyang Pan and coauthors present an integrated microcomb-optoelectronic chaos engine, iMOCE, that turns optical chaos into a massively parallel physical resource for ML acceleration. The idea is to combine the wavelength parallelism of a chaotic microcomb with the bandwidth of an optoelectronic nonlinear cavity.

The hardware advance is significant. The system generates 32 parallel chaotic channels with a 6-dB bandwidth of 25 GHz per channel, around two orders of magnitude beyond previous microcomb schemes. It also produces symmetric amplitude distributions without complex post-processing, making the chaos easier to use directly in computing tasks.

For random-bit generation, iMOCE reaches 32.768 Tbps, with 1.024 Tbps per channel. But the paper goes beyond random numbers. The same physical entropy source is used in a 1,024-armed bandit task, a connect-3 game, a Hopfield-network traveling-salesman solver and ECG arrhythmia recognition.

The results show why physical randomness can matter. In connect-3, moderate chaotic bias moves the system from suboptimal greedy strategies to a 100% win rate. In the ECG task, a photonic probabilistic neural network reaches 95.7% accuracy and can reject out-of-distribution inputs through uncertainty estimates.

The scientific point is not that optics replaces all digital computation. The point is more precise: integrated photonics can supply high-bandwidth, low-correlation, parallel stochastic signals that digital processors can use as an acceleration primitive.

For edge AI, communications, autonomous systems, secure hardware or real-time biomedical monitoring, this is a concrete example of photonic hardware entering the ML pipeline. Faster physical entropy could make exploration, probabilistic inference and stochastic optimization less of a software bottleneck.

Paper: Pan et al., Nature Communications (2026), CC BY 4.0 | https://t.co/CWIkmbyzao

In reinforcement learning, stochastic search, Hopfield optimization and probabilistic neural networks, the quality and speed of the entropy source can become part of the computing system itself. Zhouyang Pan and coauthors present an integrated microcomb-optoelectronic chaos engine, iMOCE, that turns optical chaos into a massively parallel physical resource for ML acceleration. The idea is to combine the wavelength parallelism of a chaotic microcomb with the bandwidth of an optoelectronic nonlinear cavity. The hardware advance is significant. The system generates 32 parallel chaotic channels with a 6-dB bandwidth of 25 GHz per channel, around two orders of magnitude beyond previous microcomb schemes. It also produces symmetric amplitude distributions without complex post-processing, making the chaos easier to use directly in computing tasks. For random-bit generation, iMOCE reaches 32.768 Tbps, with 1.024 Tbps per channel. But the paper goes beyond random numbers. The same physical entropy source is used in a 1,024-armed bandit task, a connect-3 game, a Hopfield-network traveling-salesman solver and ECG arrhythmia recognition. The results show why physical randomness can matter. In connect-3, moderate chaotic bias moves the system from suboptimal greedy strategies to a 100% win rate. In the ECG task, a photonic probabilistic neural network reaches 95.7% accuracy and can reject out-of-distribution inputs through uncertainty estimates. The scientific point is not that optics replaces all digital computation. The point is more precise: integrated photonics can supply high-bandwidth, low-correlation, parallel stochastic signals that digital processors can use as an acceleration primitive. For edge AI, communications, autonomous systems, secure hardware or real-time biomedical monitoring, this is a concrete example of photonic hardware entering the ML pipeline. Faster physical entropy could make exploration, probabilistic inference and stochastic optimization less of a software bottleneck. I do, it’s useful for Superconducting though not for many other of the modalities, it’s not a universal need across the quantum computing space. 

$XNDU uses photonics which manipulates light and works in room temp 
$INFQ uses neutral atoms which works in room temp, the atoms itself must be cooled but they use lasers to achieve this
$IONQ $QNT is trapped ion based and don’t need cyrostats as hardware can sit at room temp.

text:
🌐 THE FUTURE ECONOMY -> mapped
Forget the headlines. Here's the infrastructure the next decade actually runs on, and the names building each layer 🧵👇

💡 PHOTONICS - light replaces copper
$CRDO · $SIVE · $AAOI
The interconnect bottleneck of AI. Every GPU cluster moving from electrons to light.

🔷 GLASS & OPTICAL MATERIALS - the substrate light runs on
$GLW · $LPTH · $LPKF
The fiber that carries the signal, the infrared optics that shape it, the glass cores going into advanced packaging.

⚙️ AI INFRASTRUCTURE - the compute buildout
$IREN · $NBIS · $DGXX
Power, data centers, neoclouds. The physical backbone of the AI economy.

📡 CONNECTIVITY & RF - moving the signal
$AMPG
Re-shored 5G and the RF signal chain. The certified domestic alternative for 5G networks, SATCOM, and defense.

🤖 PHYSICAL AI - intelligence that acts
$OUST · $AMBA · $KRKNF
The eyes, the brain, and the body. AI moving into the real world.

🛸 AUTONOMY & DRONES - machines that operate themselves
$ONDS · $AUR · $MRLN
Aerial drones, driverless freight, autonomous flight. The re-shored defense and logistics layer.

⚡ ENERGY & STORAGE - powering the buildout
$TE · $EOSE · $SPKL
Domestic solar, long-duration grid storage, and short-duration data-center backup. The full duration stack.

🪨 CRITICAL MINERALS - the hard constraints
$MP · $UAMY · $ASPI
Rare earths, antimony, nuclear fuel. The materials China controls that the West must re-shore.

🚀 SPACE - the orbital economy
$ASTS · $RKLB · $PL
Direct-to-cell satellites and the launch backbone.
The thesis in one line: own the chokepoints and physical inputs the digital economy can't function without.

The screens get the attention. The infrastructure gets the returns.

text:
The market keeps debating the exact timing of CPO.

But the more important point is simpler:

Optical content per GPU is going up structurally.

According to Morgan Stanley, optical engines per GPU could rise from roughly 2 today to around 17 in NVL576 hybrid, about 35 in full CPO, and even 70 in a bull-case CPO architecture.

That is not a small upgrade cycle.
That is a step-change in optical content.
The key debate is not whether AI needs more optics.
It is how fast the architecture moves from copper or pluggable optics toward deeper photonics integration.

CPO timing can move around.
But the direction is clear: larger clusters, higher bandwidth, more rack-scale interconnect, and more optical engines per GPU.
This is why I still think optics remains one of the most important underpriced layers in AI infrastructure.

AI scaling is about moving data fast enough for that compute to matter.
More GPUs need more bandwidth.
More bandwidth needs more optics.

text:
Silicon photonics doesn't eliminate InP demand. It changes where InP sits in the stack. Nearly every advanced optical architecture still needs InP lasers somewhere.

That's why I remain bullish on names tied to the chokepoints rather than the finished products. 

Companies like $TSEM, $AEHR, $MTSI, $SIVE and $IQE sit much closer to the scarce parts of the supply chain.

The most overlooked point is that CPO could actually increase total InP demand. Less InP per link means little if AI factories require exponentially more optical links.

text:
People are talking a lot about AI stocks, so here's my list (with reasoning):

1) $OPTX - Pure-play precision photonics for AI data center interconnects. Bullish on strong design wins and being one of the cleanest high-moat enablers in the optics stack.

2) $ASYS - Thermal processing equipment for AI chip packaging and advanced substrates. Bullish on the clear AI revenue mix shift (already 30-40%+) and improving margins.

3) $SHMD - Advanced packaging equipment player with accelerating AI/HPC orders. Bullish on new panel-level packaging tech and strong 2026 guidance tied to AI infrastructure buildout.

4) $AAOI - AI transceiver leader with massive hyperscaler orders and rapid capacity expansion. Bullish on the $1B+ 2026 revenue guide and being one of the most direct ways to play AI optics demand.

5) $BRUN - AI cloud / GPU infrastructure play with long-term secular tailwinds. Bullish on the multi-year AI data center demand (despite near-term lock-up overhang).

6) $AMBQ - Ultra-low power edge AI SoCs using proprietary SPOT technology. Bullish on the shift of AI from data centers to on-device (wearables, medical, robotics) and its strong balance sheet.

7) $MU - Dominant HBM memory supplier with sold-out 2026 supply. Bullish because memory is now a structural bottleneck in AI clusters and MU has clear leadership in HBM3E/HBM4.

8) $SIVE - AI infrastructure beneficiary with exposure to key enablers. Bullish as an earlier asymmetric call with growth tied to AI buildout.

9) $PENG - High-quality photonics / connectivity solutions for AI infrastructure. Bullish due to excellent execution, clean fundamentals, and strong positioning in the AI connectivity layer.

10) $PDYN - Physical AI and defense autonomy platform with major new loitering munitions deal. Bullish on the IAI partnership catalyst and growing defense revenue visibility.

11) $XFAB - Specialty foundry with exposure to both photonics and power semis plus government backing. Bullish on undervalued growth in AI-critical processes and capex support.

12) $SILC - Networking and smart NIC solutions with AI inference traction. Bullish on 33%+ growth and design wins in high-performance data center connectivity.

13) $MRLN - Flight autonomy software for military and commercial aircraft. Bullish on high catalyst density (USSOCOM, FAA progress, demos) and asymmetric setup.

14) $ACCON - Physical AI radar/sensing solutions with tier-1 design wins. Bullish on de-risked backlog and being a high-conviction play on sensors for autonomous systems.

15) $AIRJ - Data center cooling solution targeting the water bottleneck. Bullish on niche but real tailwind from hyperscaler water usage constraints and early partnerships.

16) $VIVO - AI data center power and infrastructure pivot play. Bullish on the thematic exposure to AI power bottlenecks despite higher execution risk.

17) $MX - Power semiconductors for AI servers and data centers. Bullish on structural demand for efficient power delivery in AI racks.

18) $IREN - Bitcoin miner with one of the strongest AI/HPC data center pivots. Bullish on power deals and credible shift toward AI infrastructure hosting.

19) $NBIS - Emerging AI data center / infrastructure name. Bullish on early positioning in the AI power and facility buildout with asymmetry at small scale.

20) $CLSK - Bitcoin miner expanding into AI data centers with strong power assets. Bullish on the AI pivot potential while maintaining Bitcoin leverage.

There's my list.

This is in order of best -> worst, so do with that what you want.

My top 5 is what I'm mainly in here (besides my positions in $MU and $SIVE that are already up).

Would be curious to hear your guys' thoughts.

What are you buying in here?

text:
Tom's winners/losers breakdown is excellent.

One addition to the WINNERS column: $ALMU.

Reuters' core point is that AI infrastructure is running into an InP substrate and manufacturing bottleneck.

Aeluma's approach is different. They're not competing for more 3-4 inch InP wafers. They're integrating InP/GaAs photonics onto 300 mm silicon manufacturing platforms.

When the bottleneck is InP supply, companies building alternative manufacturing architectures deserve attention.
$ALMU

speculative high risk/high reward shitcos with charts (no paywall): https://t.co/wSF41NGWzj

LPTH LightPath Technologies starter position adds at bottom of flag

BFLY Butterfly Network buying dip still

WOLF - Wolfspeed

HIMX - Himax Technologies

ADTN - Adtran Holdings

HYLN - Hyliion Holdings adding on dip to UTL

POET Photonics - Currently minimal position, as it has become controversial.

SIVEF Photonics - Recently sold 1/2 my position (though it current holdings are still substantial) as I believe valuation has gotten way ahead of itself

INV AI - Owns an asset that is best in breed for 2 phase data center cooling - huge TAM market, and that Accelcius asset worth twice the current market cap of INV - I sold this one down on shortseller accusations of fudging their 1st datacenter deal.

KRKNF Defense/Robotics - Supplier to Andural, and play on Ocean defense spend - Peeled some off higher, looking to add back ..looking to add at target/s below

NRGV Energy & SMR - BESS (Battery Energy Storage Systems) data center /energy storage rebuilding of the grid play - huge TAM, continued insider buying by CEO, large position, would like to add but waiting for earnings.//UPDATE - on earnings call CEO made it clear this is a Q4/2027 story - so no add, but pleasantly surprised that stock holding up so far //Would like to add at lower bullseye (not clear I will get the chance)

TGEN Energy & SMR - Make Chillers and trying to break into the datacenter market…One hyper-scaler/neo-cloud potentially a $50M sale - partnered with Vertiv VS $150M market cap…one sale, and will re-rate. Waiting on earnings to see if any new traction

LODE Energy & SMR - Solar panel recycling -Might look to add at those rising MA’s coming up from below - nice size inside buys recently

RDW Space - Defense 2.0/Space play…P/E owner been dumping millions of shares artificially depressing the price - Peeled some off until i’m clear this is not a double top - looking to add back at bullseye if I get the opportunity // Haven’t added back yet

VELO Space - $400M Market cap - Only (?) 3D print system for larger format parts - space industry, and others….Insider owns size. looking to add at bullseye below (on UTL taps)

IQE (PF)

ASPI Energy & SMR - Finally acting well, looking to buy dips //Updated to add acting like shit again…

MYO added on Friday’s dip …position building still

AMSSY Took a placeholder position but am hoping it works it’s way down to the UTL where I want to get more aggressive…hopefully I dont miss loading up because I LOVE the risk/reward looking out a year or two

AIXI high risk - along with my all or none patent NLST …this one has a likely win in the China courts……

NLST Memory Patent litigation play…higher conviction than PRKR - but high risk/high (potential) reward never the less …Their trial was passed down to state courts from federal - no bueno

SGMO Healthcare/Bio Total SPEC / high risk…Sub .12 is sub $50M market cap VS $100’sM in product/platform/patents ….$20M cash left - worst case diluted into oblivion…will stay in for 6ish weeks - but want out before earnings likely

LPKFF Photonics I just bought the earnings dip in the 19’s - would add on any further dips

SHMD Machinery for glass substrates market (semi advanced packaging)…one of the few that can do it…small position until I see better/more market penetration outside of China…don’t like 12% margins….

LWLG Photonics - This is a late 2027 story IMO…been adding on dips

ONDS Defense/Robotics - Getting re-rated to reflect future millions/billions in sales - wrap up of multiple drone related companies… Want to add but want to make certain not a double top up here

BRUN - people smarter than I are questioning the valuation, so I have cut back accordingly….see how it acts at UTL below if it gets there

NNBR Data-center play busbars as well as connectors for chip/server-cooling. starter position adding on dips

NIS 150 million to advance Israel's next generation of photonic chips.

Together with the Ministry of Defense's Directorate of Defense Research & Development (DDR&D/MAFAT), we've launched a new initiative to establish a national R&D infrastructure for integrated photonics, supporting the path from research and prototyping to commercialization.

text:
@TomSzczypka Excellent thread — the InP export chokehold and supply-chain fragmentation angle is spot-on.

One name that belongs in the WINNERS column: $ALMU

Aeluma isn’t just another company hoping for more InP wafers. They’re integrating InP/GaAs photonics directly onto large silicon wafers (they’ve already demonstrated the world’s first 300 mm InP-on-Si).

No native InP substrate needed. No fighting China for export approvals. Scales on abundant, standard silicon infrastructure instead.

Their CEO Jonathan Klamkin just won the prestigious IPRM Award for exactly this heterogeneous integration work on mismatched substrates.

While everyone else is stuck competing for the scarce piece, $ALMU is building around it.

This is the long-term supply-chain winner when fragmentation accelerates. Add it to the list.
